Benchmark Eatery is a local business located at 1201 State St in Santa Barbara, CA. It offers a variety of cuisines including American (Traditional), American (New), Breakfast & Brunch, Seafood, Vegetarian, and more. The business has received a 4.0-star rating from customers.

The restaurant's hours of operation are from 11:30 AM to 8:00 PM on Tuesdays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays, and from 11:30 AM to 4:00 PM on Fridays and Saturdays. On Sundays, it is open from 11:30 AM to 8:00 PM. The business is closed on Mondays.

Based on customer reviews, Benchmark Eatery is described as an upscale casual place with a friendly atmosphere. Customers have enjoyed the flatbreads and clam chowder, although one customer mentioned that the chowder was a bit thick. The restaurant offers outdoor seating and free WiFi. It is also suitable for groups and provides takeout options.

However, there was a negative review mentioning issues with the food. The customer had ordered a BLAT sandwich with grilled chicken and a side salad but found the food to be bland and unseasoned. The service was average, with the customer having to ask for a soda refill. Additionally, the cleaning spray used on the tables had an unpleasant odor.

Overall, Benchmark Eatery offers a diverse menu but may benefit from focusing on one type of food to improve quality. The patio area is mentioned as a nice feature, but some customers did not find it worth returning for.

source_info.name: Benchmark Eatery
source_info.categories: American (Traditional), American (New), Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants, Seafood, Vegetarian, Nightlife, Event Planning & Services, Bars, Venues & Event Spaces

review_info.review_text: Nice little place in downtown Santa Barbara where we stopped for late lunch/early dinner on our way back home to San Diego. We loved our flatbreads. I had been craving clam chowder and ordered it here. It was a little thick for my liking, but tasted good. Nice, friendly upscale casual place on State Street.

I had high hopes for this place, but it fell flat big time. I ordered the BLAT with grilled chicken and a side salad. I specifically asked for no tomato and they put tomato on both the sandwich and the salad. Not a huge deal, just annoying to pick it all off. And if you have an allergy, I would not trust this place to be mindful of that. The grilled chicken was not seasoned at all and the food overall was just extremely bland. My sandwich was stacked so high that the avocado all fell out as I was eating it. The side salad is lettuce, cucumber, red onion, and tomato. My bf got a burger with onion rings and he did not enjoy either part of the meal. The onion rings are beer battered and basically all batter. The burger was also unseasoned and not enjoyable. The service is ok. The staff is nice enough but my bf did have to get up and ask for his soda to be refilled and we were never offered a refill on our waters. We both left the restaurant with a stomach ache, I personally felt ill. I had stomach pains for a few hours afterwards and I can only assume it's because the food is not fresh because my meal didn't have any allergens that would've given me that reaction. Lastly, the cleaning spray they used on the tables just smelled funky. Idk how to explain it but we actually moved to an outside table because the inside one smelled so musty and mildewy.

The patio is nice, but not worth coming back for. Additionally, the menu is too scattered and overwhelming. I think they should stick to one type of food and work on increasing the quality rather than having too many different menu options that aren't cohesive.

We have been here a few times during trial over the past couple weeks and it is awesome.  I keep ordering the burrito but everyone seems to enjoy their food.  The one piece of feedback would be the Spanish soup seems to change - from not too spicy to spicy.

response: Benchmark Eatery is a popular restaurant located in the heart of downtown Santa Barbara, offering a diverse menu that includes American traditional, seafood, vegetarian, and nightlife options. The restaurant has a cozy and intimate atmosphere, with both indoor and outdoor seating areas. The menu offers a wide variety of dishes, including flatbreads, salads, sandwiches, and burgers, and the restaurant is known for its delicious clam chowder and tasty grilled chicken. The staff is friendly and attentive, and the restaurant offers free Wi-Fi and take-out options. Some customers have noted that the food can be a bit hit or miss, with some dishes being flavorful and others lacking seasoning. Additionally, some customers have reported issues with food allergies not being properly accommodated. However, overall, Benchmark Eatery is a great spot for a casual meal or gathering in Santa Barbara.

source_info.name: Benchmark Eatery
source_info.categories: American (Traditional), American (New), Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants, Seafood, Vegetarian, Nightlife, Event Planning & Services, Bars, Venues & Event Spaces

review_info.review_text: Nice little place in downtown Santa Barbara where we stopped for late lunch/early dinner on our way back home to San Diego. We loved our flatbreads. I had been craving clam chowder and ordered it here. It was a little thick for my liking, but tasted good. Nice, friendly upscale casual place on State Street.

I had high hopes for this place, but it fell flat big time. I ordered the BLAT with grilled chicken and a side salad. I specifically asked for no tomato and they put tomato on both the sandwich and the salad. Not a huge deal, just annoying to pick it all off. And if you have an allergy, I would not trust this place to be mindful of that. The grilled chicken was not seasoned at all and the food overall was just extremely bland. My sandwich was stacked so high that the avocado all fell out as I was eating it. The side salad is lettuce, cucumber, red onion, and tomato. My bf got a burger with onion rings and he did not enjoy either part of the meal. The onion rings are beer battered and basically all batter. The burger was also unseasoned and not enjoyable. The service is ok. The staff is nice enough but my bf did have to get up and ask for his soda to be refilled and we were never offered a refill on our waters. We both left the restaurant with a stomach ache, I personally felt ill. I had stomach pains for a few hours afterwards and I can only assume it's because the food is not fresh because my meal didn't have any allergens that would've given me that reaction. Lastly, the cleaning spray they used on the tables just smelled funky. Idk how to explain it but we actually moved to an outside table because the inside one smelled so musty and mildewy.

The patio is nice, but not worth coming back for. Additionally, the menu is too scattered and overwhelming. I think they should stick to one type of food and work on increasing the quality rather than having too many different menu options that aren't cohesive.

We have been here a few times during trial over the past couple weeks and it is awesome.  I keep ordering the burrito but everyone seems to enjoy their food.  The one piece of feedback would be the Spanish soup seems to change - from not too spicy to spicy.

response: Pizza Mizza is an Italian restaurant located in Santa Barbara, California, known for its food delivery services. The restaurant offers a variety of pizzas, pastas, salads, and more, with numerous choices and sizes, including gluten-free and cauliflower crust options. It is open from 11:00 to 21:00 on Mondays and Sundays and has a casual ambiance. The restaurant offers free Wi-Fi, outdoor seating, and is good for groups, but does not provide music. However, the restaurant has a rating of 3.5 stars, with some customers praising the quality of the food, but criticizing the length of time it takes for food to arrive and the level of customer service. The restaurant also offers takeout and has a parking lot, but does not offer street parking, valet, or validated parking.

source_info.name: Pizza Mizza
source_info.categories: Food Delivery Services, Food, Restaurants, Pizza, Italian

review_info.review_text: Bummed only delivery now probably my favorite pizza in town everywhere else sucks and are making restaurants you feel like you almost have to dress up for now and over priced including the owners of Pizza Mizza new pizza restaurant on state

This pizza place is pretty average. They have a huge list of different kinds of pizzas, pastas, salads, and more! I recently tried to call an order in to pick up but was told they only do delivery right now so I ended up just doing delivery. I couldn't decide what to order with all of their different choices and sizes! They even have cauliflower and gluten free crust options!

I ended up getting the personal size Hawaiian pizza with a normal crust. It took about an hour to arrive at my house but when I finally got it I was so happy and hungry. The pineapple was fresh, along with the ham and the crust was cooked with the perfect amount of crisp! They also gave me multiple sides of ranch which I was stoked about since most places always never actually bring the ranch when you order it or give you only one.

They also gave me plates and cheese along with the hot peppers! Overall I had a great experience here getting delivery here and I can't wait to order them again! :)

I always wondered why this place had such average to low rating and after a few times of visiting this place, I can see why. Food is great! I have no complaints over their food. There used to be a Pizza Mizza in Ventura and I remember going there often so when I saw there was one in SB, I had to go give them a try.

As for customer service here...
I'm always left wondering if there's anyone in charge here or if any of the front staff even care about their job. This isn't my first time waiting here over 30minutes for food which is beyond ridiculous. I've ordered different things and they all take so long. I don't know if they're just short staffed but this is not acceptable in any restaurant business especially if this is a continuous problem. My boyfriend ordered a sandwich which they left sitting on their rack for 10min while my pasta was nowhere to be seen. I was sick of their tendency to just hang out, have conversations amongst each other, and not care about their hungry customers. I went to the front and asked them if that plate on the rack was for us and they said that it just came out and were waiting for my pasta to come out. That sandwich did NOT JUST come out but obviously they were too busy with their conversations to realize it was there. Whatever, I brought it to my table and we waited another 10 minutes for my pasta. I saw two plates again sitting on the rack for about 5mins and I told myself if none of those are mine, I'm going to leave. Well it turns out none of them were mine and one server who was aware of our situation came to notify us that due to the delay, he had refunded my entire bill. Great. So I'm still hungry. The food is most likely going to take more time. I got my money back. I'm not waiting anymore. So my boyfriend and I left. 

The food is so good that I would've given this place 5 stars but unfortunately, the staff is a big miss. Maybe management should train their staff again or hire people who actually care about their job.

review_info.review_text: Bummed only delivery now probably my favorite pizza in town everywhere else sucks and are making restaurants you feel like you almost have to dress up for now and over priced including the owners of Pizza Mizza new pizza restaurant on state

This pizza place is pretty average. They have a huge list of different kinds of pizzas, pastas, salads, and more! I recently tried to call an order in to pick up but was told they only do delivery right now so I ended up just doing delivery. I couldn't decide what to order with all of their different choices and sizes! They even have cauliflower and gluten free crust options!

I ended up getting the personal size Hawaiian pizza with a normal crust. It took about an hour to arrive at my house but when I finally got it I was so happy and hungry. The pineapple was fresh, along with the ham and the crust was cooked with the perfect amount of crisp! They also gave me multiple sides of ranch which I was stoked about since most places always never actually bring the ranch when you order it or give you only one.

They also gave me plates and cheese along with the hot peppers! Overall I had a great experience here getting delivery here and I can't wait to order them again! :)

I always wondered why this place had such average to low rating and after a few times of visiting this place, I can see why. Food is great! I have no complaints over their food. There used to be a Pizza Mizza in Ventura and I remember going there often so when I saw there was one in SB, I had to go give them a try.

As for customer service here...
I'm always left wondering if there's anyone in charge here or if any of the front staff even care about their job. This isn't my first time waiting here over 30minutes for food which is beyond ridiculous. I've ordered different things and they all take so long. I don't know if they're just short staffed but this is not acceptable in any restaurant business especially if this is a continuous problem. My boyfriend ordered a sandwich which they left sitting on their rack for 10min while my pasta was nowhere to be seen. I was sick of their tendency to just hang out, have conversations amongst each other, and not care about their hungry customers. I went to the front and asked them if that plate on the rack was for us and they said that it just came out and were waiting for my pasta to come out. That sandwich did NOT JUST come out but obviously they were too busy with their conversations to realize it was there. Whatever, I brought it to my table and we waited another 10 minutes for my pasta. I saw two plates again sitting on the rack for about 5mins and I told myself if none of those are mine, I'm going to leave. Well it turns out none of them were mine and one server who was aware of our situation came to notify us that due to the delay, he had refunded my entire bill. Great. So I'm still hungry. The food is most likely going to take more time. I got my money back. I'm not waiting anymore. So my boyfriend and I left. 

The food is so good that I would've given this place 5 stars but unfortunately, the staff is a big miss. Maybe management should train their staff again or hire people who actually care about their job.

response: Taffy's Pizza, located at 2026 De La Vina St, Santa Barbara, CA, is a popular local eatery. The restaurant, which is open from Tuesday to Sunday, has a 4-star business rating. It is known for its extensive pizza menu, but also offers a wide variety of other foods including salads, pastas, and sandwiches. The restaurant has a casual ambience and is good for groups. It offers free Wi-Fi and outdoor seating. Parking is available both on the street and in a lot. Though some customers have had issues with payment methods, many others praise the restaurant's excellent customer service, delicious food, and family-friendly atmosphere. They even have a small arcade room for children. The restaurant offers free delivery, but customers are advised to place their orders early due to high demand.

source_info.name: Taffy's Pizza
source_info.categories: Restaurants, Pizza

review_info.review_text:
Bought a axxess card, they refused to accept it. Don't order the
chicken piccata, there., they declined it,,   Especially, when you go to pay, you pay full price. So why did I get the card, and pay $ 40.00
Will I go back,?  well as a first time customer, chances are. ZERO
No second chances, and I would have been a steady customer.

We love, love, love Taffy's! We've had almost everything on the menu and have enjoyed every bite. There's no doubt about it that Taffy's is our favorite pizza in town, but we also love the great beer selection, bread sticks, salads, pastas and sandwiches. 

They have a HUGE selection of pizza breads, veggies toppings, cheeses and meats. Because they have such a variety of toppings, their sandwiches, salads, etc. have a great selection of toppings too. I called and accidentally asked to order an antipasto salad, not realizing that they don't even have an antipasto salad on the menu. However, they have such amazing customer service, that they assured me they have everything to make a great antipasto salad delivered to my house. And they were right!! It was incredible. 

AND free delivery, but call early to place your order. It can take a little while depending on when you call. So worth it for free delivery!! 

Pre-Covid times, they offered fun events like trivia on their large, private outdoor patio. I hope they can start that up again soon. 

They also have a small arcade room and lots of children's books scattered here and there for the kiddos. Very family friendly!

Fantastic pizza. Have been coming to taffy's for 20 years since I was a teenager and now bringing my kids here. Love it. Good food at reasonable price. Classic arcade in the back with some of the same games for the last 20 years.

Taffy's Pizza is a local business located at 2026 De La Vina St in Santa Barbara, CA. It falls under the categories of Restaurants and Pizza. The business has specific operating hours, opening from 11:00 AM to 9:00 PM from Tuesday to Saturday, and from 12:00 PM to 8:00 PM on Sundays. On Mondays, the business remains closed. 

Taffy's Pizza offers various amenities and features. It has parking options available on the street and in a nearby lot. The restaurant also provides outdoor seating and offers free WiFi to its customers. Reservations can be made, and takeout services are available. Taffy's Pizza is known for being suitable for groups and has a casual ambience.

Customers have left mixed reviews about their experiences at Taffy's Pizza. One customer expressed dissatisfaction, mentioning issues with accepting a purchased axxess card and the chicken piccata dish. They seemed unlikely to return as a first-time customer. On the other hand, another customer gave a glowing review, praising the wide variety of menu options, including pizza, breadsticks, salads, pastas, and sandwiches. They lauded the customer service and the ability to customize their order. Free delivery was also mentioned as a positive aspect. The customer highlighted the family-friendly environment and previous fun events held at the business, such as trivia nights and the availability of an arcade room.

Overall, Taffy's Pizza has received an average rating of 4.0 stars. Some customers have had negative experiences, while others have been long-time patrons and appreciate the delicious pizza and reasonable prices.

review_info.review_text:
Bought a axxess card, they refused to accept it. Don't order the
chicken piccata, there., they declined it,,   Especially, when you go to pay, you pay full price. So why did I get the card, and pay $ 40.00
Will I go back,?  well as a first time customer, chances are. ZERO
No second chances, and I would have been a steady customer.

We love, love, love Taffy's! We've had almost everything on the menu and have enjoyed every bite. There's no doubt about it that Taffy's is our favorite pizza in town, but we also love the great beer selection, bread sticks, salads, pastas and sandwiches. 

They have a HUGE selection of pizza breads, veggies toppings, cheeses and meats. Because they have such a variety of toppings, their sandwiches, salads, etc. have a great selection of toppings too. I called and accidentally asked to order an antipasto salad, not realizing that they don't even have an antipasto salad on the menu. However, they have such amazing customer service, that they assured me they have everything to make a great antipasto salad delivered to my house. And they were right!! It was incredible. 

AND free delivery, but call early to place your order. It can take a little while depending on when you call. So worth it for free delivery!! 

Pre-Covid times, they offered fun events like trivia on their large, private outdoor patio. I hope they can start that up again soon. 

They also have a small arcade room and lots of children's books scattered here and there for the kiddos. Very family friendly!

Fantastic pizza. Have been coming to taffy's for 20 years since I was a teenager and now bringing my kids here. Love it. Good food at reasonable price. Classic arcade in the back with some of the same games for the last 20 years.

response:
"Sam's To Go" is a well-rated sandwich restaurant located at 315 Meigs Rd, Ste I, Santa Barbara, CA. This restaurant specializes in American (New) cuisine and is open every day of the week. The establishment features outdoor seating and offers free WiFi. It provides takeout options and is suitable for group dining, though it does not accept reservations. Convenient lot parking is available, but garage, street, and valet parking are not. 

Customer reviews highlight the excellent customer service and high-quality ingredients used in the sandwiches, with particular praise for the Italian cold cuts and veggie sandwich. However, some customers have expressed disappointment with the quality and taste of certain sandwiches, such as the turkey sandwich, along with some instances of perceived poor customer service. Despite some negative reviews, the overall star rating for "Sam's To Go" remains high at 4.5 stars.

source_info.name: Sam's To Go
source_info.categories: Sandwiches, Restaurants, American (New)

review_info.review_text: This was our first time at Sams and it was delicious!! Excellent customer service, they were so sweet. We got the #11 Italian cold cuts, and the veggie sandwich #29 both were so good! The bread was so fresh and you could tell the ingredients were really quality! My daughter got the turkey sandwich and she is the pickiest eater and she loved it! Can't wait to go back

The couple who runs the place are so nice and funny, and the sandwiches are delicious. It doesn't look fancy, but do yourself a favor and try it out. The Pastrami Avocado and Cheese was delicious. Warning, the medium size sandwich is BIG.

I've been going to Sam's for the past several years while visiting beautiful Santa Barbara from the San Francisco Bay area. I've always enjoyed the sandwiches from Sam's. Unfortunately, my wife and I just finished eating their turkey sandwich and in all honesty, it' was just, Ok, more like disappointing...

We were disappointed because it appears the quality and taste of the sandwich has digressed to a "point of no return"... The sandwich was sloppily made, poorly wrapped, and importantly, the man behind the counter (usual guy) seems to have gotten cantankerous over time. He chastised me for not ordering both white and wheat bread sandwiches the way he wanted, getting perturbed for asking for olives in my sandwich and, he had a smart alec - bad attitude demeanor about him - poor customer service.

Suffice it to say, we will not be going back to Sam's. There are too many good delis in the Cliffs Dr. area to be subjected to poor quality sandwiches and an unpleasant customer experience...

response:
Sam's To Go is a local business located at 315 Meigs Rd, Ste I in Santa Barbara, CA. It is a sandwich restaurant that also serves American (New) cuisine. The business is open from 10:00 AM to 9:00 PM from Monday to Saturday, and from 10:00 AM to 7:30 PM on Sundays. The establishment offers free WiFi and has outdoor seating available. Customers have the option to take out their orders, and the restaurant is also good for groups.

Based on customer reviews, Sam's To Go has received positive feedback. Customers have praised the delicious sandwiches and excellent customer service. The ingredients used in the sandwiches are of high quality, and the bread is fresh. The #11 Italian cold cuts and the veggie sandwich #29 are highly recommended. The restaurant is run by a friendly and funny couple. Despite its casual appearance, the food is tasty, and the Pastrami Avocado and Cheese sandwich is particularly recommended.

However, there is one negative review mentioning a decline in the quality and taste of the sandwiches. The customer had a disappointing experience with a poorly made and wrapped turkey sandwich. The staff member behind the counter exhibited a bad attitude and provided poor customer service.

Overall, Sam's To Go has a rating of 4.5 stars and offers a range of delicious sandwiches with friendly service.

response: Viva Modern Mexican, located at 1114 State St in Santa Barbara, CA, is a venue that offers a variety of dining options including Breakfast & Brunch, Peruvian, Latin American, Seafood, Tapas/Small Plates, and Mexican cuisines. They also offer event planning services. The restaurant provides free wifi, outdoor seating, and takeout services. Their ambiance is classy without being too trendy or casual. Despite having an overall rating of 3.5 stars, customer reviews are mixed. source_info.name: Viva Modern Mexican
source_info.categories: Breakfast & Brunch, Venues & Event Spaces, Peruvian, Event Planning & Services, Bars, Nightlife, Latin American, Restaurants, Cocktail Bars, Seafood, Tapas/Small Plates, Mexican

review_info.review_text: so sad !!!! It was so good. If you open somewhere else will you let your fans know ! Loved your margarita flights ( the best fresh margaritas I have ever had ) and light fresh,flavorful  salads.

My husband got really bad food poisoning from this place. We ate there Friday evening and he's been vomiting etc since. He is currently in the ER for treatment. To top it off they dropped a plate and glass flew everywhere and cut my ankle.

We had our rehearsal dinner and welcome party here in July and it was so perfect!! We rented the upstairs space for the evening and had 30 guests for the rehearsal dinner and then the rest of the guests (about 75 people) joined after for drinks. The taco buffet was delicious!!! And the space was exactly what we wanted for our group.  Dina, the Events Coordinator, was incredibly helpful throughout our planning process, starting in January and right through the event itself. All of the staff here were amazing. I highly recommend this restaurant for anyone looking to host a similar event, or even for dinner - the food was amazing!!

response: Hollister Brewing Company, located at 6980 Marketplace Dr, Goleta, CA, is a local business that specializes in American traditional food and beer. It is a brewpub that operates from 11:30 to 19:30 from Monday to Sunday, with extended hours until 20:00 on Fridays and Saturdays. The establishment offers outdoor seating and free WiFi, accepts reservations, and is good for groups. It also provides a takeout service and has a parking lot. Customer reviews highlight mixed feelings towards the business. Some patrons express satisfaction with the new menu introduced by a new chef, praising dishes like the Korean chicken wings and the homemade dressings. However, other customers have criticized the slow service and staff behavior, including drinking on the job. The business has an average rating of 3.5 stars.

source_info.name: Hollister Brewing Company
source_info.categories: Restaurants, Food, Breweries, American (Traditional), Brewpubs

review_info.review_text: I miss the old menu a lot! The staff here isn't as friendly and the bartenders drink on the job here including there bar manager! Where's Tom at to take control! Saw the manager tana take shots with employees and completely ignoring guest! Not okay!

So, so, so excited to have new chef and brand new menu. The favorites from the old still remain but they have been kicked up a notch in flavor. It was such a pleasant surprise and long overdue.

I get the Korean chicken wings. I can honestly say they are the best at Hollister and now the chicken comes juicy, not dry, and tons of flavor.

I love that Hollister makes their own dressings. I do like getting a salad every once in awhile.

My hubs was actually pleased with the change and finally enjoys eating there. We will be there today!

Hollister is conveniently located in a large shopping area and has an array of beers they brew right in the restaurant. Full bar and some wine to choose from.

Hollister has been a staple with my family and we are so happy there's a new menu. Give it a try!!

Waited 30min until asked to take our drink & food order. 
Very slow service, if your hungry don't expect to eat soon you will be waiting along time. We had kids and already sat down or else we would of left .

review_info.review_text: I miss the old menu a lot! The staff here isn't as friendly and the bartenders drink on the job here including there bar manager! Where's Tom at to take control! Saw the manager tana take shots with employees and completely ignoring guest! Not okay!

So, so, so excited to have new chef and brand new menu. The favorites from the old still remain but they have been kicked up a notch in flavor. It was such a pleasant surprise and long overdue.

I get the Korean chicken wings. I can honestly say they are the best at Hollister and now the chicken comes juicy, not dry, and tons of flavor.

I love that Hollister makes their own dressings. I do like getting a salad every once in awhile.

My hubs was actually pleased with the change and finally enjoys eating there. We will be there today!

Hollister is conveniently located in a large shopping area and has an array of beers they brew right in the restaurant. Full bar and some wine to choose from.

Hollister has been a staple with my family and we are so happy there's a new menu. Give it a try!!

Waited 30min until asked to take our drink & food order. 
Very slow service, if your hungry don't expect to eat soon you will be waiting along time. We had kids and already sat down or else we would of left .

response: Jeannine's American Bakery Restaurant is a traditional American bakery and restaurant located at 7060 Hollister Ave, Ste 105, in Goleta, CA. The establishment offers a variety of food categories including breakfast and brunch, coffee and tea. The restaurant operates from 7:30 am to 1:30 pm from Wednesday to Friday, and until 2 pm on Saturdays and Sundays. It offers free WiFi, outdoor seating, and take-out options, with a focus on a classy ambience. The restaurant has a business rating of 4.5 stars. Reviews are mixed, with customers praising the French toast, ambiance, service, and fresh orange juice. However, some customers were dissatisfied with the coffee and other dishes, stating they were not up to the standard expected. The restaurant also offers parking lots, but no valet, garage or street parking.

source_info.name: Jeannine's American Bakery Restaurant
source_info.categories: Bakeries, Restaurants, Food, American (Traditional), Breakfast & Brunch, Coffee & Tea

review_info.review_text: I am super disappointed. I love the Jeannine's in Montecito. But this one is just not that great. Coffee is not great , I think the drink coffee is a good indicator on how serious a place takes their coffee and this place does not do great coffee. I ordered chilequiles and we super excited since I love that dish most places I go to but tbh it was the worst chilequiles I've ever had. The taste was bitter and even my dad who eats everything and is not picky didn't eat it. Also I asked specifically not to overlook my eggs, and they were overcooked. Bummer! Paid 7 for fresh OJ... also not tasty. Ordered French toast for my boy and it was definitely the best thing we ordered. I also got a croissant and I know this is brutal but Costco makes them better. Sorry guys I hate to be so negative but to spend 65 bucks on breakfast that was a MAJOR let down. I will not be going back.

Visiting from SF - this place is excellent. Don't be out off by it's location in a mall. 

The food and service are top notch and the decor inside is super cute. 

We had the French toast and Avo toast with matcha lattes made with oat milk. 

Everything was made perfectly and was really delicious. The eggs had deep Orange yolks and were also super tasty. 

Two thumbs up!

I absolutely love the ambiance, and the attentive service.
The french toast was delicious!
Fresh OJ gets a two thumbs up!
Now the bacon! I'd go there just for that! 
Smokey and crisp!
My friend had that All American and she was pleased!
Definitely on repeat! 
Thanks to everyone for a nice experience!

response: Holdren's Grill, located at 6920 Market Pl Dr, Goleta, CA, offers a variety of American and seafood dishes, and is also known for nightlife and as a steakhouse. It provides amenities like free WiFi, outdoor seating, and parking lot, also catering to group dining and takeouts. However, it holds an average rating of 2.5 stars. Customers are divided on their experiences. Some find the food good and service excellent, lauding the wide variety of choices on the menu, with dishes ranging from crab-cakes to artichoke dips to filet mignons. However, others report negative experiences, citing unattentive service, overpriced menu options, and issues with payment processing. There are also complaints about the quality of the food, with specific mentions of undercooked fish and burnt steaks. The establishment is open every day, from 4 PM to 9 or 10 PM.

source_info.name: Holdren's Grill
source_info.categories: Seafood, American (New), Restaurants, Nightlife, Steakhouses, American (Traditional), Bars

review_info.review_text: I have to give this restaurant a 1-star rating purely due to horrible service. While the extremely pricey menu options were surprisingly good, the service in the whole restaurant was awful! Our server came to our table twice the entire night. Once to seat us, and once after we waited a very long time to get our check. He never came to check on us or ask how our food was even once! One table next to us even walked out before getting their food, or maybe they just never received it. A couple sat at the hostess stand for a good 10 mins before anyone greeted them as well. I'm surprised they didn't leave! It's too bad that the staff was so un attentive, because the food was actually pretty good! We will probably not be returning here:/

I frequent this location often on the weekends as a hang-out location with my friends. Over the many time I have been here, I have had a consistent experience with the food, the environment, as well as the service. 

When you open the menu, you will be quite surprised at the wide variety of choices available. Dishes range from crab-cakes to artichoke dips to filet mignons. Personally, I have tried the steaks many times at Holdrens, as well as a few of their select appetizers. The food is good for the price, typically around $20-$30 per individual (Combination of entree and appetizer). To me, having frequented many steakhouses in the United States such as Mastro's and Strip House, I would have to say that the quality and presentation of the slab of meat is on par with my expectations. It might be a little bit on the high side in terms of the food, but what can you expect, it is Santa Barbara. I expect the price to be more catered to the location when looking at the prices. Overall, I was happy with my food because it was delicious and I left the restaurant a happy customer. 

Many people on Yelp have been complaining about the poor service and lack of attention they received during their experience. During my few times at Holdren's, I have yet to encounter any problems. The service is excellent and attentive, and my table was always served and cared for properly. 

Overall, my experience at Holdren's was pleasant. I understand that it is located near Santa Barbara, which is prime real-estate. I expect many restaurants to institute a premium in their prices because of this. As a happy customer, I will continue to frequent this location with my friends when I do have the chance.

My family went there for my brother's birthday. The wait staff was polite and courteous but the food was terrible. The steaks were burnt and the fish was undercooked. My mom and I split the bill and they ended up charging me for the entire bill!  I spoke with the manager a few days later and she said she would refund the portion I didn't owe, and instead CHARGED me again. Thank god I had enough money in my account to cover the charges. My bill was $86 and instead I got charged twice for a total of $411.08. Ridiculous, they lost me and my family's business forever.

Holdren's Grill is a local restaurant located at 6920 Market Pl Dr in Goleta, CA. It offers a variety of cuisines including seafood, American (New), steakhouses, and American (Traditional). The restaurant has both indoor and outdoor seating options and provides free WiFi for its customers. It is open from 4:00 pm to 9:00 pm on weekdays and from 4:00 pm to 10:00 pm on weekends.

Customers have had mixed experiences at Holdren's Grill. One customer gave it a 1-star rating due to poor service. They mentioned that the server was inattentive and did not check on them or ask about the food. Another table next to them even left before receiving their food. However, this customer did mention that the food was surprisingly good.

On the other hand, another customer had a positive experience at Holdren's Grill. They mentioned that the food is delicious and the service is excellent and attentive. They were particularly impressed with the quality and presentation of the steaks. The customer also acknowledged that the prices might be on the higher side due to the location in Santa Barbara.

However, another negative review mentioned a terrible experience with the food and billing. The steaks were burnt and the fish was undercooked. Additionally, there was an issue with the billing where the customer was charged twice for a total of $411.08 instead of the $86 bill.

Overall, Holdren's Grill has received mixed reviews from customers, with some praising the food and service, while others have had negative experiences.

review_info.review_text: I have to give this restaurant a 1-star rating purely due to horrible service. While the extremely pricey menu options were surprisingly good, the service in the whole restaurant was awful! Our server came to our table twice the entire night. Once to seat us, and once after we waited a very long time to get our check. He never came to check on us or ask how our food was even once! One table next to us even walked out before getting their food, or maybe they just never received it. A couple sat at the hostess stand for a good 10 mins before anyone greeted them as well. I'm surprised they didn't leave! It's too bad that the staff was so un attentive, because the food was actually pretty good! We will probably not be returning here:/

I frequent this location often on the weekends as a hang-out location with my friends. Over the many time I have been here, I have had a consistent experience with the food, the environment, as well as the service. 

When you open the menu, you will be quite surprised at the wide variety of choices available. Dishes range from crab-cakes to artichoke dips to filet mignons. Personally, I have tried the steaks many times at Holdrens, as well as a few of their select appetizers. The food is good for the price, typically around $20-$30 per individual (Combination of entree and appetizer). To me, having frequented many steakhouses in the United States such as Mastro's and Strip House, I would have to say that the quality and presentation of the slab of meat is on par with my expectations. It might be a little bit on the high side in terms of the food, but what can you expect, it is Santa Barbara. I expect the price to be more catered to the location when looking at the prices. Overall, I was happy with my food because it was delicious and I left the restaurant a happy customer. 

Many people on Yelp have been complaining about the poor service and lack of attention they received during their experience. During my few times at Holdren's, I have yet to encounter any problems. The service is excellent and attentive, and my table was always served and cared for properly. 

Overall, my experience at Holdren's was pleasant. I understand that it is located near Santa Barbara, which is prime real-estate. I expect many restaurants to institute a premium in their prices because of this. As a happy customer, I will continue to frequent this location with my friends when I do have the chance.

My family went there for my brother's birthday. The wait staff was polite and courteous but the food was terrible. The steaks were burnt and the fish was undercooked. My mom and I split the bill and they ended up charging me for the entire bill!  I spoke with the manager a few days later and she said she would refund the portion I didn't owe, and instead CHARGED me again. Thank god I had enough money in my account to cover the charges. My bill was $86 and instead I got charged twice for a total of $411.08. Ridiculous, they lost me and my family's business forever.

response: Uniboil is a trendy, casual restaurant located at 5599 Hollister Ave, Unit C in Goleta, California, specializing in Asian Fusion, Hot Pot, Chinese food, and Bubble Tea. The restaurant offers both indoor and outdoor dining, with free Wi-Fi and a lot for parking. Open from 11:30 to 20:30 from Tuesday to Sunday, Uniboil accepts reservations and is suitable for groups. The restaurant is known for its hotpots and has a wide range of dishes. It also offers a variety of boba drinks, alongside its connected business, Phresh Tea. Despite some inconsistency in customer service and occasional shortages of specific dishes, the restaurant maintains an average rating of 4 stars. Customers commend the Beef Thai Tom Yum Hotpot, the sizzling beef dish, and popcorn chicken but have mixed opinions about the beef noodle soup.

source_info.name: Uniboil
source_info.categories: Food, Bubble Tea, Chinese, Restaurants, Asian Fusion, Hot Pot

review_info.review_text:
I ate here three times already.

Parking is easy; there are plenty of spaces. They have both indoor and outdoor dining. The restaurant is connected to Phresh Tea (located on the right), and both use the same kitchen, so you can order Boba or anything on Phresh Tea menu while dining at Uniboil. 

Uniboil are known for their hotpots. They also have other dishes on the menu if you're not into that. The prices are reasonable, and the hotpot portion is huge; you could probably share it with another person if you aren't too hungry. 

I really enjoyed the Beef Thai Tom Yum Hotpot. I've also had the Korean Army Stew the first time my family and I went, and it was good; I do prefer the Thai one better though. I've tried the sizzling beef dish (forgot what is called), popcorn chicken, and beef noodle soup. Sizzling beef and popcorn chicken were fantastic. I didn't like the beef noodle soup. The broth tasted fine, but the meats were terrible. I didn't have any meats in my bowl actually; it was like just fatty parts. 

Customer service has been inconsistent; sometimes they're slow, and other times they're quick. Staffs are friendly though. They checked up on us all the time.

Their boba drink is surprisedly good, I have a high standard for boba, their boba is definitely above average. Try their kumquat drink, or milk drink with less sweet ( it could be really sweet ). As for their food, I ordered a noodle soup for my son, the soup is good but not the round noodle they use. Order their drink if you visit.

Felt weird vibes here. Was confused that it was also attached to the tea place next door and all the workers were more situated there. Thus it was a while and took effort to get a table at the restaurant side. Even tho there was only one other party there... 

We came for the beef noodle soup and coffee. The soup Is the first picture on Yelp and looked delicious. But upon ordering the waitress said they didn't have it. They had run out. Plus they didn't have coffee either. At the end we decided not to stay

The restaurant has both indoor and outdoor seating, making it convenient for customers who prefer either option. Parking is easy with plenty of spaces available in the parking lot.

Customers have mentioned that Uniboil is known for their hot pots, which come in generous portions and are reasonably priced. The Beef Thai Tom Yum Hotpot is highly recommended, although there are other dishes on the menu for those who may not be interested in hot pots. Overall, Uniboil is a local business that offers a diverse menu with a focus on hot pots and boba drinks. While there may be some inconsistencies in service, customers generally enjoy the food and appreciate the friendly staff.

We came for the beef noodle soup and coffee. The soup Is the first picture on Yelp and looked delicious. But upon ordering the waitress said they didn't have it. They had run out. Plus they didn't have coffee either. At the end we decided not to stay

According to customer reviews, the restaurant is known for its large portions and affordable prices, with many customers recommending the Beef Thai Tom Yum Hotpot and sizzling beef dishes. Some customers have noted that the customer service can be inconsistent, with some experiences being slower than others. Uniboil offers both indoor and outdoor seating and has free WiFi available. review_info.review_text:
I ate here three times already.

Parking is easy; there are plenty of spaces. They have both indoor and outdoor dining. The restaurant is connected to Phresh Tea (located on the right), and both use the same kitchen, so you can order Boba or anything on Phresh Tea menu while dining at Uniboil. 

Uniboil are known for their hotpots. They also have other dishes on the menu if you're not into that. The prices are reasonable, and the hotpot portion is huge; you could probably share it with another person if you aren't too hungry. 

I really enjoyed the Beef Thai Tom Yum Hotpot. I've also had the Korean Army Stew the first time my family and I went, and it was good; I do prefer the Thai one better though. I've tried the sizzling beef dish (forgot what is called), popcorn chicken, and beef noodle soup. Sizzling beef and popcorn chicken were fantastic. I didn't like the beef noodle soup. The broth tasted fine, but the meats were terrible. I didn't have any meats in my bowl actually; it was like just fatty parts. 

Customer service has been inconsistent; sometimes they're slow, and other times they're quick. Staffs are friendly though. They checked up on us all the time.

Their boba drink is surprisedly good, I have a high standard for boba, their boba is definitely above average. Try their kumquat drink, or milk drink with less sweet ( it could be really sweet ). As for their food, I ordered a noodle soup for my son, the soup is good but not the round noodle they use. Order their drink if you visit.

Felt weird vibes here. Was confused that it was also attached to the tea place next door and all the workers were more situated there. Thus it was a while and took effort to get a table at the restaurant side. Even tho there was only one other party there... 

We came for the beef noodle soup and coffee. The soup Is the first picture on Yelp and looked delicious. But upon ordering the waitress said they didn't have it. They had run out. Plus they didn't have coffee either. At the end we decided not to stay

response: La Salsa Fresh Mexican Grill is a popular Mexican, fast food, and Tex-Mex restaurant located at 3987 State St, Ste D, Santa Barbara, CA. The restaurant offers ample lot parking and is open from 11 AM to 9 PM on Tuesday to Friday, and until 8 PM on Saturday and Sunday. It features outdoor seating and take-out services, making it a convenient spot for groups. However, it does not offer WiFi and does not take reservations. The ambiance is casual, as reflected in the customer reviews. The restaurant has a business star rating of 4.0. Customer reviews are mixed, with patrons praising the friendly staff and the quick service. Some customers have highlighted the delicious burritos and the classic quesadilla that comes with guacamole and sour cream. However, one review criticized the quality and flavor of the food. The restaurant is also praised for its reasonable pricing compared to similar establishments.

source_info.name: La Salsa Fresh Mexican Grill
source_info.categories: Mexican, Fast Food, Tex-Mex, Restaurants

review_info.review_text: Oh my goodness, the staff here is so nice and the food is ready so quickly! Options to sit inside or outside. I ordered the classic quesadilla and it comes with guac and sour cream! My friend got the combo burrito and soup. Such a great and convenient spot!

This spot is the worst Mexican food I have ever tasted. The food has no flavor what so ever and their meat is low quality and taste so bland. I will not come here again and would not recommend it to anyone

Delicious burritos! Fairly priced in contrast with other similar restaurants. People who work there are friendly and accommodating. response: Spudnuts & Bagels is a highly-rated restaurant located at 3629 State St, Santa Barbara, California. It operates extensive hours from Monday to Sunday and is popular for its wide selection of donuts and bagels. The restaurant has received a 4.5-star business rating. Customers have complimented its homemade cream cheese flavors, including vegetable and strawberry, which are said to be unique. Some customers believe their bagels are superior to New York bagels, with one noting the perfect combination of an everything bagel with vegetable cream cheese and lox. Others highlighted their crispy yet airy bagels and the ham and cheese-filled croissant. The restaurant offers a parking lot but doesn't provide garage, street, validated, or valet parking. Other attributes such as outdoor seating, WiFi, reservations and suitability for groups were not specified.

source_info.name: Spudnuts & Bagels
source_info.categories: Restaurants, Bagels, Coffee & Tea, Food, Bubble Tea, Donuts

review_info.review_text: One of our favorite places to grab breakfast! They have a wide selection of donuts and their bagels are always tasty. I especially enjoy their homemade cream cheese flavors - I haven't had anything like them! Their vegetable cream had chopped up veggies in it and their strawberry cream cheese had jam. Definitely stop by to try some delicious bagel and cream cheese combo and donuts!

So happy to have stumbled upon this bagel place. I've had hundreds of NY bagels of the years but I'm telling you something about the bagels is special. I ordered the everything bagel with vegetable cream cheese and lox. Literal perfection. Had to stop again before we left.

Was in SB for the phish show 10/26. Trey and the boys laid it down, but the reason I was able to rokk was Spudnuts and Bagels. Had 3 of my 5 meals there while in town. The bagels are crispy but somehow airy not chewy! The ham and cheese filled croissant - get 2 or 3. The lady who was helping me was super nice and took the time to answer all my questions. When she found out i was flying her goods home to the fam she gave me some xtras she wanted them to try - thank you S&B!!

response: Mala Town is a Chinese restaurant located at 6555 Pardall Rd, Goleta, CA. It is open from Monday to Sunday, but has varying hours of operation. The restaurant offers outdoor seating but does not provide parking facilities. The ambiance is not categorized as trendy, upscale, or casual, and it does not offer WiFi or music. The restaurant has a 4-star rating. A review from November 22, 2019, praises the large patio, the spicy soup noodles, and the generous portions. However, the reviewer was disappointed with the lack of nice utensils and received their meal with a small plastic spoon and disposable wooden chopsticks. Another reviewer, from November 13, 2019, found the food to be average and the prices high for the quality provided. A review from May 28, 2019, also commented on the high prices.

source_info.name: Mala Town
source_info.categories: Chinese, Restaurants

review_info.review_text:
A friend and I ate lunch at Mala Town today. We arrived at noon and were the only ones there. The large and lush patio was great since the weather was nice, and my bowl of spicy soup noodles hit the spot. 

I used to live in Hong Kong and have traveled in Asia quite a bit. While I can't quite call what I ate "traditional" in some respects, it was delicious. For around $11.80, I got a big bowl of tasty mala soup (at just the right spice level after I asked for it "da la" 大麻) with four ingredients. 

I chose vermicelli, broccoli, Chinese cabbage, and tofu rolls. Each was super tasty! I was a little disappointed as I had asked the friendly cashier/server if the tofu roll was the crispy beancurd roll from hotpot and he said yes, but it wasn't. It was more like rolled tofu skin--still tasty though. The broccoli was fresh and perfectly cooked, and the cabbage soaked up the delicious mala oils while the vermicelli provided the "umph" to the dish. The soup came topped with sliced spring onions and a generous dollop of chili sauce with sesame seeds. 

A little while into our meal, the server came over with two cups of mung bean soup for my friend and I. He said the chef had decided to make up a batch and gave it to us on the house. It was a nice balance of sweet to match the spicy soup, and had a liberal serving of perfectly cooked mung beans at the bottom. 

The only downside was the place's lack of nice utensils. Though I ordered my meal "for here," it came with a small plastic spoon and disposable wooden chopsticks. I carry my own chopsticks, so no worries there (BYO people, be green!), but the spoon was sad. I want to eat a lovely soup like that with a proper Chinese soup spoon that has room for me to arrange the perfect bite with my chopsticks. The silly little plastic one just didn't cut it. Despite that, I thoroughly enjoyed my lunch, and ate every bite. I think I'll be back; maybe I can try the jian bing (Chinese crepe) next time.

The food here is ok but not great, and kind of expensive for what it is. We tried both the mala tang and the dry pot (peanut-based sauce). The base is free, and you pay for the items you chose to add in (minimum of 4). They assemble your hot pot or dry hot pot in the kitchen before bringing it out in a regular bowl. The mala soup base was not very numbing, and the flavors of the base didn't seem very incorporated into the mix-ins.

They also have jian bing and some appetizers, which we didn't try--some of their other items may be better.
I believe the restaurant will not last long, even their food is not bad, because the price is much higher than expected.

Mala Town is a local Chinese restaurant located at 6555 Pardall Rd in Goleta, California. The restaurant has received an average rating of 4.0 stars from customers. The hours of operation are Monday to Thursday from 5:00 PM to 9:00 PM, Friday from 11:00 AM to 10:00 PM, and Saturday to Sunday from 12:00 PM to 10:00 PM.

Customers have praised the restaurant for its large and lush patio, which provides a great dining experience when the weather is nice. One customer mentioned that their bowl of spicy soup noodles was delicious and satisfying. For around $11.80, they received a big bowl of mala soup with four ingredients, including vermicelli, broccoli, Chinese cabbage, and tofu rolls. Each ingredient was described as super tasty. The soup came topped with sliced spring onions and a generous dollop of chili sauce with sesame seeds. 

However, there were some downsides mentioned in the reviews. One customer mentioned that the restaurant lacked nice utensils, with only a small plastic spoon and disposable wooden chopsticks provided. Another customer felt that the food was expensive for what it was and that the flavors of the base in the mala soup didn't seem well incorporated into the mix-ins.

Overall, Mala Town is a Chinese restaurant in Goleta that offers a pleasant dining experience with a variety of dishes, including spicy soup noodles and mala tang. However, some customers have expressed concerns about the pricing and the integration of flavors in the dishes.

review_info.review_text:
A friend and I ate lunch at Mala Town today. We arrived at noon and were the only ones there. The large and lush patio was great since the weather was nice, and my bowl of spicy soup noodles hit the spot. 

I used to live in Hong Kong and have traveled in Asia quite a bit. While I can't quite call what I ate "traditional" in some respects, it was delicious. For around $11.80, I got a big bowl of tasty mala soup (at just the right spice level after I asked for it "da la" 大麻) with four ingredients. 

I chose vermicelli, broccoli, Chinese cabbage, and tofu rolls. Each was super tasty! I was a little disappointed as I had asked the friendly cashier/server if the tofu roll was the crispy beancurd roll from hotpot and he said yes, but it wasn't. It was more like rolled tofu skin--still tasty though. The broccoli was fresh and perfectly cooked, and the cabbage soaked up the delicious mala oils while the vermicelli provided the "umph" to the dish. The soup came topped with sliced spring onions and a generous dollop of chili sauce with sesame seeds. 

A little while into our meal, the server came over with two cups of mung bean soup for my friend and I. He said the chef had decided to make up a batch and gave it to us on the house. It was a nice balance of sweet to match the spicy soup, and had a liberal serving of perfectly cooked mung beans at the bottom. 

The only downside was the place's lack of nice utensils. Though I ordered my meal "for here," it came with a small plastic spoon and disposable wooden chopsticks. I carry my own chopsticks, so no worries there (BYO people, be green!), but the spoon was sad. I want to eat a lovely soup like that with a proper Chinese soup spoon that has room for me to arrange the perfect bite with my chopsticks. The silly little plastic one just didn't cut it. Despite that, I thoroughly enjoyed my lunch, and ate every bite. I think I'll be back; maybe I can try the jian bing (Chinese crepe) next time.

The food here is ok but not great, and kind of expensive for what it is. We tried both the mala tang and the dry pot (peanut-based sauce). The base is free, and you pay for the items you chose to add in (minimum of 4). They assemble your hot pot or dry hot pot in the kitchen before bringing it out in a regular bowl. The mala soup base was not very numbing, and the flavors of the base didn't seem very incorporated into the mix-ins.

They also have jian bing and some appetizers, which we didn't try--some of their other items may be better.
I believe the restaurant will not last long, even their food is not bad, because the price is much higher than expected.

response: Mala Town is a Chinese restaurant located in Goleta, California, offering a variety of dishes including mala soup, dry pot, and jian bing. The restaurant has a cozy atmosphere with a large and lush patio, and offers outdoor seating. The menu includes vegetarian options, and the restaurant is open for lunch and dinner. According to customer reviews, the food at Mala Town is delicious, with a generous portion size and fresh ingredients. However, some customers have noted that the prices are a bit high compared to other restaurants in the area. One reviewer also mentioned that the utensils provided were not up to their standards, specifically requesting a proper Chinese soup spoon. Overall, Mala Town seems to be a solid option for those looking for Chinese cuisine in the Goleta area, but customers may want to consider the pricing when deciding where to dine.

source_info.name: Mala Town
source_info.categories: Chinese, Restaurants

review_info.review_text:
A friend and I ate lunch at Mala Town today. We arrived at noon and were the only ones there. The large and lush patio was great since the weather was nice, and my bowl of spicy soup noodles hit the spot. 

I used to live in Hong Kong and have traveled in Asia quite a bit. While I can't quite call what I ate "traditional" in some respects, it was delicious. For around $11.80, I got a big bowl of tasty mala soup (at just the right spice level after I asked for it "da la" 大麻) with four ingredients. 

I chose vermicelli, broccoli, Chinese cabbage, and tofu rolls. Each was super tasty! I was a little disappointed as I had asked the friendly cashier/server if the tofu roll was the crispy beancurd roll from hotpot and he said yes, but it wasn't. It was more like rolled tofu skin--still tasty though. The broccoli was fresh and perfectly cooked, and the cabbage soaked up the delicious mala oils while the vermicelli provided the "umph" to the dish. The soup came topped with sliced spring onions and a generous dollop of chili sauce with sesame seeds. 

A little while into our meal, the server came over with two cups of mung bean soup for my friend and I. He said the chef had decided to make up a batch and gave it to us on the house. It was a nice balance of sweet to match the spicy soup, and had a liberal serving of perfectly cooked mung beans at the bottom. 

The only downside was the place's lack of nice utensils. Though I ordered my meal "for here," it came with a small plastic spoon and disposable wooden chopsticks. I carry my own chopsticks, so no worries there (BYO people, be green!), but the spoon was sad. I want to eat a lovely soup like that with a proper Chinese soup spoon that has room for me to arrange the perfect bite with my chopsticks. The silly little plastic one just didn't cut it. Despite that, I thoroughly enjoyed my lunch, and ate every bite. I think I'll be back; maybe I can try the jian bing (Chinese crepe) next time.

The food here is ok but not great, and kind of expensive for what it is. We tried both the mala tang and the dry pot (peanut-based sauce). The base is free, and you pay for the items you chose to add in (minimum of 4). They assemble your hot pot or dry hot pot in the kitchen before bringing it out in a regular bowl. The mala soup base was not very numbing, and the flavors of the base didn't seem very incorporated into the mix-ins.

They also have jian bing and some appetizers, which we didn't try--some of their other items may be better.
I believe the restaurant will not last long, even their food is not bad, because the price is much higher than expected.

response: Alchemy Wellness Spa, located at 35 W Haley St, Santa Barbara, CA, offers a variety of services including skin care, fitness instruction, vegan and vegetarian dining, acupuncture, and massage. The business operates from Monday to Sunday, with extended hours on Thursday. The establishment provides free WiFi and is good for groups. There is street and lot parking available, but no valet service. The spa holds a business star rating of 4.0. Many customers have praised the spa for its impressive services, particularly the float tank and the facials. They also commend the heart-centered service and welcoming environment. However, a review highlighted some disappointment with the massage service, citing inadequate pressure and cold oil. Despite this, the spa's cafe received positive feedback.

source_info.name: Alchemy Wellness Spa
source_info.categories: Skin Care, Cafes, Fitness & Instruction, Vegan, Vegetarian, Active Life, Restaurants, Acupuncture, Health & Medical, Massage, Day Spas, Beauty & Spas, Weight Loss Centers, Yoga

review_info.review_text: Gorgeous setting for treatments and a cup of tea ~ if you've never tried a float tank before, I highly recommend it! Great way to meditate and relax. I was particularly impressed, however, with the lovely, heart-centered service. When I needed a place to nurse and change my infant I was beyond accommodated and made to feel very welcome. What a lovely experience!

I would highly recommend Danielle for facials to everyone I know. She did a phenomenal job and I came out looking much younger. The entire experience was fabulous from the front counter to the beautiful environment. Great job all thank you very much.

Horrible massage. We came here looking for a great massage and all we got was good decor. The cafe was good (between the two of us, we had coffee, tea, and oatmeal - oatmeal was good but nothing special). 

We had Lizzie who was supposed to "the best" massage therapist, but she definitely wasn't:
-The oil she used was cold - not a pleasant surprise to start off the massage.
-I got "deep tissue" ...I had to tell her twice for more pressure - shouldn't have to ask for that for a deep tissue massage. Even after asking for more pressure, she didn't do it. Come to find out, the person I came with who got a Swedish massage said Lizzie's pressure was weak and limp as well! From the way she timidly handled my head and neck to how I could uncomfortably feel her nails in my skin, everything screamed that Lizzie had either never given a massage before or was extremely inexperienced. 

SUCH a disappointment considering we were looking forward to a great massage AND considering we paid $135 EACH. Big disappointment.

I was particularly impressed, however, with the lovely, heart-centered service. When I needed a place to nurse and change my infant I was beyond accommodated and made to feel very welcome. What a lovely experience!

I would highly recommend Danielle for facials to everyone I know. She did a phenomenal job and I came out looking much younger. The entire experience was fabulous from the front counter to the beautiful environment. Great job all thank you very much.

Horrible massage. We came here looking for a great massage and all we got was good decor. The cafe was good (between the two of us, we had coffee, tea, and oatmeal - oatmeal was good but nothing special). 

We had Lizzie who was supposed to "the best" massage therapist, but she definitely wasn't:
-The oil she used was cold - not a pleasant surprise to start off the massage.
-I got "deep tissue" ...I had to tell her twice for more pressure - shouldn't have to ask for that for a deep tissue massage. Even after asking for more pressure, she didn't do it. Come to find out, the person I came with who got a Swedish massage said Lizzie's pressure was weak and limp as well! From the way she timidly handled my head and neck to how I could uncomfortably feel her nails in my skin, everything screamed that Lizzie had either never given a massage before or was extremely inexperienced. 

SUCH a disappointment considering we were looking forward to a great massage AND considering we paid $135 EACH. Big disappointment.

response:
Alchemy Wellness Spa is a luxurious wellness center located in the heart of Santa Barbara, offering a range of treatments and services designed to promote relaxation, rejuvenation, and overall well-being. With a focus on holistic health and wellness, the spa features a team of experienced practitioners who deliver personalized care and attention to each client.

The spa's atmosphere is tranquil and serene, with a beautifully appointed interior that creates a sense of calm and relaxation. Clients can enjoy a variety of treatments, including massages, facials, and body treatments, as well as take advantage of the spa's float tank and infrared sauna. The spa also offers a selection of healthy snacks and drinks, including organic coffee and tea, and delicious oatmeal.

Reviews of the spa are largely positive, with many clients praising the friendly and attentive staff, as well as the peaceful and relaxing atmosphere. One reviewer notes that the spa's float tank is a unique and enjoyable experience, while another reviewer raves about the exceptional service and personalized attention received during a facial treatment. However, one reviewer expresses disappointment with a recent massage experience, citing lack of pressure and an unpleasant, cold oil used during the treatment.

Overall, Alchemy Wellness Spa appears to be a popular destination for those seeking a luxurious and relaxing wellness experience in Santa Barbara. While there may be some variability in the quality of certain treatments, the spa's focus on holistic health and wellness, combined with its tranquil atmosphere and personalized service, make it an attractive option for individuals looking to unwind and rejuvenate.

response:
Blaze Pizza is a fast food restaurant located at 6546 Pardall Rd, Goleta, CA. The restaurant specializes in Pizza, Gluten-Free, Salad and offers an outdoor seating option. Blaze Pizza operates from 11:00 am to 8:00 pm from Monday to Sunday, with extended hours until 9:00 pm on Friday and Saturday. They do not take reservations and do not provide Wi-Fi. Street parking is available and the restaurant is suitable for group dining. 

Customer reviews indicate a mixed experience. Some customers appreciate the reasonably priced pizzas with unlimited toppings and the convenience of the location for UCSB students. However, some customers have expressed concerns about cleanliness and reported negative experiences with staff behavior. The restaurant has been praised for its safety measures during the pandemic. Overall, Blaze Pizza has a business rating of 3.5 stars.

source_info.name: Blaze Pizza
source_info.categories: Pizza, Gluten-Free, Salad, Restaurants, Fast Food

review_info.review_text:
Their pizzas are quite nice. Unlimited toppings for a personal size pizza is a model I personally love, especially since their pizzas are priced reasonably at just under $10. While Blaze is inferior to Pieology, it offers a nice alternative that is closer to campus for UCSB students. In terms of taste, Pieology is slightly better with their dough in that they brush it with a layer of oil before hand so the crust turns out more tasty and less dry. However, Blaze Pizza still taste great overall, especially if you take the time to build your own to your liking. Their Lemonade is also very nicely flavored. 

One issue I have with the store is that the floor is incredibly sticky during the time I went, which raises concerns about cleanliness.

I just want to warn everyone that NO ONE in Blaze was wearing gloves and one person who came in from the back kept putting their mask on and off near the food. 

I used to come here all the time during the school year, but today, I just had such a terrible experience. The man making our pizza made a snarky remark when I asked him for a onions and was quite hostile when I was confused about placing the card in the container instead of handing it to him during the transaction. 

Blaze needs to get their act together or they can easily get shut down due to these conditions and the behavior their employees have towards their customers.

Very grateful for blaze pizza to be open during this time. The workers wore their mask during work, and every time they made a new pizza, they wore a different glove to ensure there is no contamination. I felt very welcomed and comfortable here and the portions they give for the pizza toppings were very generous. Overall, thankful and would come back!

review_info.review_text:
Their pizzas are quite nice. Unlimited toppings for a personal size pizza is a model I personally love, especially since their pizzas are priced reasonably at just under $10. While Blaze is inferior to Pieology, it offers a nice alternative that is closer to campus for UCSB students. In terms of taste, Pieology is slightly better with their dough in that they brush it with a layer of oil before hand so the crust turns out more tasty and less dry. However, Blaze Pizza still taste great overall, especially if you take the time to build your own to your liking. Their Lemonade is also very nicely flavored. 

One issue I have with the store is that the floor is incredibly sticky during the time I went, which raises concerns about cleanliness.

I just want to warn everyone that NO ONE in Blaze was wearing gloves and one person who came in from the back kept putting their mask on and off near the food. 

I used to come here all the time during the school year, but today, I just had such a terrible experience. The man making our pizza made a snarky remark when I asked him for a onions and was quite hostile when I was confused about placing the card in the container instead of handing it to him during the transaction. 

Blaze needs to get their act together or they can easily get shut down due to these conditions and the behavior their employees have towards their customers.

Very grateful for blaze pizza to be open during this time. The workers wore their mask during work, and every time they made a new pizza, they wore a different glove to ensure there is no contamination. I felt very welcomed and comfortable here and the portions they give for the pizza toppings were very generous. Overall, thankful and would come back!

response: Live Oak Cafe, located at 2220 Bath St, Santa Barbara, CA, is a versatile establishment offering a variety of food options and services. With categories ranging from Fast Food and American (New) to Wine Bars and Breakfast & Brunch, it operates different hours on weekdays and weekends. The cafe offers amenities such as free WiFi, outdoor seating, and group-friendly dining. It also accommodates take-outs and reservations. According to customer reviews, the cafe serves excellent breakfasts such as eggs, omelettes, and delicious coffee. It also offers healthy and tasty options like skillets with eggs and a kale/spinach/quinoa side. The cafe's unique dishes like fried rice with in-house cured bacon, pork buns, and bacon waffles have been highly commended. Furthermore, customers appreciate the convenient location near Cottage Hospital and Sansum Clinic, ample parking, and the quiet, comfortable atmosphere. Live Oak Cafe has earned a business star rating of 4.5.

source_info.name: Live Oak Cafe
source_info.categories: Farmers Market, Food, Fast Food, American (New), Bars, Wine Bars, Breakfast & Brunch, Cafes, Restaurants, Nightlife

review_info.review_text:
We had an excellent breakfast today at the Live Oak Cafe. My eggs and my wife's omelet were done just right. The coffee was hot & fresh. I should have thought to ask the brand name of the coffee because it was delicious.
Our server was also very good and the food was delivered to our table hot, which in some restaurants is a challenge.
This restaurant is very convenient to Cottage Hospital, the Sansum Clinic and other surrounding medical offices.
I highly recommend giving The Live Oak Cafe a try.

Absolutely loved my breakfast. I got a skillet with eggs, kale/spinach/quinoa side and it was oh so tasty. My best friend and I were on the way to the Santa Barbara Mission from LA and we left really early thinking there would be a ton of traffic. Much to our surprise, it wasn't that bad so we decided to stop by and grab a bite to eat. The location is real easy to pass by, but there was plenty of parking, and no one was there which was nice. I definitely want to stop by again because the food was healthy, tasty, and fresh!

What a surprising treat! Came here for lunch to get quality food and avoid crowds. The atmosphere is quiet, comfy and green. The service was great and oh the FRIED RICE! It's delicious with their in house cured bacon. Highly recommend. The pork buns were a special treat to find and very good. The bacon waffle was delicious as well, made with high quality ingredients. It was a great experience.

response:
Located in the heart of Santa Barbara, Live Oak Cafe offers a delightful dining experience with its farm-to-table cuisine, cozy atmosphere, and exceptional service. As suggested by its name, the cafe is nestled under the shade of a live oak tree, providing a tranquil setting for patrons to enjoy their meals. The restaurant boasts an impressive 4.5-star rating and has received rave reviews from satisfied customers.

Breakfast and brunch are the cafe's specialties, with dishes that blend traditional flavors with creative twists. The menu features a variety of options, including egg dishes, sandwiches, salads, and sides, all made with fresh, locally sourced ingredients. Coffee lovers will appreciate the expertly brewed coffee, which is made with high-quality beans.

The cafe's ambiance is casual and inviting, with comfortable seating and plenty of natural light. Outdoor seating is also available, offering a pleasant spot to enjoy your meal amidst the beautiful surroundings. Additionally, the cafe provides free Wi-Fi, making it an excellent choice for those looking for a relaxed workspace or a place to catch up with friends.

The staff at Live Oak Cafe is friendly and attentive, ensuring that every guest has a positive experience. Reservations are accepted, and take-out is available for those who prefer to enjoy their meals off-premises. Moreover, the cafe is well-suited for groups and offers a convenient location near several medical offices and hospitals, making it a great option for a quick bite before or after appointments.

In summary, Live Oak Cafe is a hidden gem in Santa Barbara, offering delicious farm-to-table cuisine, a cozy atmosphere, and outstanding service. Whether you're in the mood for a hearty breakfast, a healthy lunch, or a relaxed dining experience, this cafe is sure to impress.

review_info.review_text: Always the best place for breakfast in Carpenteria. Mimosas are the best. Service is stellar. You can't go wrong if you randomly choose something off the daily specials menu. I love this place!

This is my favorite restaurant. It's a must have whenever my family and I are in Carpinteria. The entire staff is incredibly nice and there isn't a bad thing on the menu. source_info.name: Winchell's Donuts
source_info.categories: Food, Donuts, Restaurants, Sandwiches, Coffee & Tea

review_info.review_text: I love this place I do but when I walked in and saw fly's on the donuts I was devastated. Why!!!???

I'm sorry i hate to do this. But I just bough 6 donuts here today that were so stale and dried out. I threw.all but 1 away.   The chocolate old fashioned was so dry I couldn't even get thru one bite.   I get that there is so much less traffic.   But then make less at a time and make them again in the afternoon.   The maple  buttermilk bar weighed a ton and again was so dry.  Usually the apple fritter can save the day... but no go, unfortunately.  Of the six I bought the only one I was able to eat was the chocolate crueller.

I will say the girl who helped me was very friendly and was wearing a mask and washed her hands. So at least there's that!
The donuts were okay and the store was not the cleanest. The customer service was good nothing special but it wasn't bad customer service. But overall it was a okay experience

I'm sorry i hate to do this. But I just bough 6 donuts here today that were so stale and dried out. I threw.all but 1 away.   The chocolate old fashioned was so dry I couldn't even get thru one bite.   I get that there is so much less traffic.   But then make less at a time and make them again in the afternoon.   The maple  buttermilk bar weighed a ton and again was so dry.  Usually the apple fritter can save the day... but no go, unfortunately.  Of the six I bought the only one I was able to eat was the chocolate crueller.

I will say the girl who helped me was very friendly and was wearing a mask and washed her hands. So at least there's that!
The donuts were okay and the store was not the cleanest. The customer service was good nothing special but it wasn't bad customer service. But overall it was a okay experience
