response: The Ritz-Carlton Bacara, Santa Barbara, located at 8301 Hollister Ave, is a multi-faceted establishment offering a range of services including restaurants, beauty and spa services, event planning, and hotel accommodation. The property is known for its beautiful setting and great service, as well as its good food and pleasant ambiance. Despite its remote location away from downtown, it makes for a compelling stay with various activities such as beach hiking and biking around the hotel area. However, some guests have reported issues with room booking and amenities, such as TV malfunctions. The hotel seeks to resolve these issues by providing compensations like restaurant fees. The property also offers valet parking and free Wi-Fi. Despite a few hiccups, most guests had a pleasant stay and would recommend the hotel, with particular praise for its comfortable rooms, fantastic gym facilities, great views, and delicious food options. The Ritz-Carlton Bacara holds a business star rating of 3.5.

source_info.name: The Ritz-Carlton Bacara, Santa Barbara
source_info.categories: Restaurants, Beauty & Spas, American (New), Event Planning & Services, Hotels, Massage, Resorts, Day Spas, Hotels & Travel, Venues & Event Spaces

review_info.review_text: Beautiful setting. Nice addition to the chain. Great service by Jon! Food was good. And the ambiance was nice too.

Only a solid THREE STARS. 

I will put in details for my review here. This review is not biased, pure 100%  our experience staying at Ritz Carlton Bacara Santa Barbara. 

Ritz Carlton SB is not located in a prime area. This is a resort,  their property is isolated away from downtown so that customers can enjoy the activities that the resort provides, such as hiking by the beach, biking around the hotel area. Mind you, this hotel is not walkable to restaurants in SB, they provided their own chain restaurants to make guests enjoy and relax at the property.

Here is our experience:
Like any other experienced travellers, we booked our hotels through credit card partners using our mileage. Who pay their hotel rooms in full nowadays anyways? Most smart people pay their hotel rooms with points or mileage affiliated with credit cards company. Originally we booked for double beds. However a day before, I received an email from them that my room was changed to single bed. This is quite impossible to our situation since we have 2 adults and 2 children. So I called (many times) to our credit card company and to Ritz to make sure we would get 2 double beds instead of 1 single king bed. The number of phone calls to change the number of beds did not happen only in one call, unfortunately. I had to call them several times before our arrival. 

So, arrival day, we got our 2 double beds. Perfect. Checking in was a smooth process. The receptionist was polite, explaining us everything about the resort. By the way, the resort has so many activities that you could book first through their website. And I also appreciate the concierge emailed us days before reminding us about the activities on the days we stay. (Tips: the receptionist offered us a transportation like a luggage cart to transport the guests to your villas, ACCEPT IT! Ritz carlton property is so huge that you can even get lost in the area. You can walk if you want to reach your 10k steps, though). 

When we got to our hotel, problem arise. Tv did not work. I called the receptionist, and the technician came after an hour later. They tried their best to fix tv, unfortunately the problem was much bigger than they expected. The sad part was they KNEW the tv was broken already and Ritz didn't even solve the problem. (Technician told me our previous guest also had the same problem - broken tv). So, me just being me, asking for a compensation for the broken tv. The technician was very generous giving us $100 restaurant fee.  And I took it with my grateful heart. 

Resort wise; this property again has a lot activities that you can do with your kids, partners, etc. Hiking by the beach is the most popular activities done by my people. Biking around the area also a great thing to do. 

During check out time, there was a bit problem since I wanted to redeem the $100 certificate that the technician gave me. A bit miscommunication happened at that time, but they resolved it quickly.

Would I recommend you guys to stay here? if you have $1000 extra to spare for staying a night here, sure you should. 
Don't forget to add
Parking vallet is $53 a day
Resort fee is $50 a day. (that's why maximise the property since you're charged hefty for resort fee). 

Other than that, yes I would stay again here. Hopefully I can bump this review to 4 star next time.

Amazing hotel with such such comfy rooms. We had a iv and there was an area right outside the room where he could go in the morning..so so convenient..there was a small lawn with a gorgeous fountain right outside the room which was awesome. The GYM WAS BEYOND FANTASTIC!!! the views were great. Service was great at the coffee shop and the bar. Room service we got it in the lobby was great. THE YUCCA FRIES ARE A MUST Have!!! The chips and guacamole were totally yum! We got takeout in the lobby and hung out which was so fun! Having a fridge and microwave in the room was so helpful. We will definitely come back. Highly recommend!

The Ritz-Carlton Bacara, Santa Barbara is a local business located at 8301 Hollister Ave in Santa Barbara, CA. It offers a range of services and amenities including restaurants, beauty & spas, event planning & services, hotels, massage, resorts, day spas, hotels & travel, and venues & event spaces.

Customers have praised the beautiful setting and the addition of this establishment to the Ritz-Carlton chain. They have also highlighted the great service provided by Jon, the good food, and the pleasant ambiance.

However, one customer mentioned that the location of the hotel is not in a prime area and is isolated from downtown Santa Barbara. The hotel provides its own chain restaurants and activities for guests to enjoy, such as hiking and biking. The customer also mentioned some issues with their room, including a broken TV, but appreciated the compensation provided by the hotel.

Despite these issues, the resort offers a variety of activities for guests to enjoy, including hiking by the beach and biking. The hotel has comfortable rooms, a fantastic gym, and great service at the coffee shop and bar. Guests have also praised the convenience of having a fridge and microwave in the room.

Overall, while there have been some minor issues, The Ritz-Carlton Bacara, Santa Barbara provides a pleasant experience with its amenities and services.

source_info.categories: Restaurants, Beauty & Spas, American (New), Event Planning & Services, Hotels, Massage, Resorts, Day Spas, Hotels & Travel, Venues & Event Spaces

review_info.review_text: Beautiful setting. Nice addition to the chain. Great service by Jon! Food was good. And the ambiance was nice too.

Only a solid THREE STARS. 

I will put in details for my review here. This review is not biased, pure 100%  our experience staying at Ritz Carlton Bacara Santa Barbara. 

Ritz Carlton SB is not located in a prime area. This is a resort,  their property is isolated away from downtown so that customers can enjoy the activities that the resort provides, such as hiking by the beach, biking around the hotel area. Mind you, this hotel is not walkable to restaurants in SB, they provided their own chain restaurants to make guests enjoy and relax at the property.

Here is our experience:
Like any other experienced travellers, we booked our hotels through credit card partners using our mileage. Who pay their hotel rooms in full nowadays anyways? Most smart people pay their hotel rooms with points or mileage affiliated with credit cards company. Originally we booked for double beds. However a day before, I received an email from them that my room was changed to single bed. This is quite impossible to our situation since we have 2 adults and 2 children. So I called (many times) to our credit card company and to Ritz to make sure we would get 2 double beds instead of 1 single king bed. The number of phone calls to change the number of beds did not happen only in one call, unfortunately. I had to call them several times before our arrival. 

So, arrival day, we got our 2 double beds. Perfect. Checking in was a smooth process. The receptionist was polite, explaining us everything about the resort. By the way, the resort has so many activities that you could book first through their website. And I also appreciate the concierge emailed us days before reminding us about the activities on the days we stay. (Tips: the receptionist offered us a transportation like a luggage cart to transport the guests to your villas, ACCEPT IT! Ritz carlton property is so huge that you can even get lost in the area. You can walk if you want to reach your 10k steps, though). 

When we got to our hotel, problem arise. Tv did not work. I called the receptionist, and the technician came after an hour later. They tried their best to fix tv, unfortunately the problem was much bigger than they expected. The sad part was they KNEW the tv was broken already and Ritz didn't even solve the problem. (Technician told me our previous guest also had the same problem - broken tv). So, me just being me, asking for a compensation for the broken tv. The technician was very generous giving us $100 restaurant fee.  And I took it with my grateful heart. 

Resort wise; this property again has a lot activities that you can do with your kids, partners, etc. Hiking by the beach is the most popular activities done by my people. Biking around the area also a great thing to do. 

During check out time, there was a bit problem since I wanted to redeem the $100 certificate that the technician gave me. A bit miscommunication happened at that time, but they resolved it quickly.

Would I recommend you guys to stay here? if you have $1000 extra to spare for staying a night here, sure you should. 
Don't forget to add
Parking vallet is $53 a day
Resort fee is $50 a day. (that's why maximise the property since you're charged hefty for resort fee). 

Other than that, yes I would stay again here. Hopefully I can bump this review to 4 star next time.

Amazing hotel with such such comfy rooms. We had a iv and there was an area right outside the room where he could go in the morning..so so convenient..there was a small lawn with a gorgeous fountain right outside the room which was awesome. The GYM WAS BEYOND FANTASTIC!!! the views were great. Service was great at the coffee shop and the bar. Room service we got it in the lobby was great. THE YUCCA FRIES ARE A MUST Have!!! The chips and guacamole were totally yum! We got takeout in the lobby and hung out which was so fun! Having a fridge and microwave in the room was so helpful. We will definitely come back. Highly recommend!

review_info.review_text: Beautiful setting. Nice addition to the chain. Great service by Jon! Food was good. And the ambiance was nice too.

Only a solid THREE STARS. 

I will put in details for my review here. This review is not biased, pure 100%  our experience staying at Ritz Carlton Bacara Santa Barbara. 

Ritz Carlton SB is not located in a prime area. This is a resort,  their property is isolated away from downtown so that customers can enjoy the activities that the resort provides, such as hiking by the beach, biking around the hotel area. Mind you, this hotel is not walkable to restaurants in SB, they provided their own chain restaurants to make guests enjoy and relax at the property.

Here is our experience:
Like any other experienced travellers, we booked our hotels through credit card partners using our mileage. Who pay their hotel rooms in full nowadays anyways? Most smart people pay their hotel rooms with points or mileage affiliated with credit cards company. Originally we booked for double beds. However a day before, I received an email from them that my room was changed to single bed. This is quite impossible to our situation since we have 2 adults and 2 children. So I called (many times) to our credit card company and to Ritz to make sure we would get 2 double beds instead of 1 single king bed. The number of phone calls to change the number of beds did not happen only in one call, unfortunately. I had to call them several times before our arrival. 

So, arrival day, we got our 2 double beds. Perfect. Checking in was a smooth process. The receptionist was polite, explaining us everything about the resort. By the way, the resort has so many activities that you could book first through their website. And I also appreciate the concierge emailed us days before reminding us about the activities on the days we stay. (Tips: the receptionist offered us a transportation like a luggage cart to transport the guests to your villas, ACCEPT IT! Ritz carlton property is so huge that you can even get lost in the area. You can walk if you want to reach your 10k steps, though). 

When we got to our hotel, problem arise. Tv did not work. I called the receptionist, and the technician came after an hour later. They tried their best to fix tv, unfortunately the problem was much bigger than they expected. The sad part was they KNEW the tv was broken already and Ritz didn't even solve the problem. (Technician told me our previous guest also had the same problem - broken tv). So, me just being me, asking for a compensation for the broken tv. The technician was very generous giving us $100 restaurant fee.  And I took it with my grateful heart. 

Resort wise; this property again has a lot activities that you can do with your kids, partners, etc. Hiking by the beach is the most popular activities done by my people. Biking around the area also a great thing to do. 

During check out time, there was a bit problem since I wanted to redeem the $100 certificate that the technician gave me. A bit miscommunication happened at that time, but they resolved it quickly.

Would I recommend you guys to stay here? if you have $1000 extra to spare for staying a night here, sure you should. 
Don't forget to add
Parking vallet is $53 a day
Resort fee is $50 a day. (that's why maximise the property since you're charged hefty for resort fee). 

Other than that, yes I would stay again here. Hopefully I can bump this review to 4 star next time.

Amazing hotel with such such comfy rooms. We had a iv and there was an area right outside the room where he could go in the morning..so so convenient..there was a small lawn with a gorgeous fountain right outside the room which was awesome. The GYM WAS BEYOND FANTASTIC!!! the views were great. Service was great at the coffee shop and the bar. Room service we got it in the lobby was great. THE YUCCA FRIES ARE A MUST Have!!! The chips and guacamole were totally yum! We got takeout in the lobby and hung out which was so fun! Having a fridge and microwave in the room was so helpful. We will definitely come back. Highly recommend!

response:
The Ritz-Carlton Bacara, Santa Barbara is a luxury resort located on the coast of Santa Barbara, offering stunning ocean views and a range of amenities. The resort features a variety of activities, including hiking, biking, and a spa, as well as several dining options. Guests can enjoy the beautifully landscaped gardens and take advantage of the resort's private beach. The hotel's rooms are stylishly decorated and equipped with modern amenities, such as flat-screen TVs and free Wi-Fi. Some rooms have ocean views, and suites are also available. The resort offers valet parking and a resort fee, which includes access to the fitness center, pool, and other amenities.

Reviews of the resort are generally positive, with guests praising the beautiful setting, comfortable rooms, and excellent service. One reviewer noted that the resort's location is not within walking distance of downtown Santa Barbara, but the resort's shuttle service makes it easy to get around. Another reviewer appreciated the resort's activities and amenities, such as the spa and fitness center, but noted that the TV in their room was broken and took some time to be fixed. Overall, the Ritz-Carlton Bacara, Santa Barbara is a great option for travelers looking for a luxurious and relaxing getaway in a beautiful coastal setting.

response: Campus Point Coffee, located at 6560 Pardall Rd, Goleta, CA, is a popular local cafe with a 4.5-star rating. It offers a variety of categories including food, cafes, coffee & tea, bakeries, and restaurants. Customers appreciate the availability of both indoor and outdoor seating along with free WiFi. Their menu features a wide variety of drinks, including a unique coconut charcoal mocha, and food items like vegan cookies and empanadas. Customers find the staff friendly and the cafe's atmosphere conducive for studying. It's also noted for its provision of bathroom facilities. Campus Point Coffee regularly posts special offers and discounts on their social media platforms. However, there is no parking available at the premises. The cafe does not specify whether it offers takeout or if it's suitable for groups.

source_info.name: Campus Point Coffee
source_info.categories: Food, Cafes, Coffee & Tea, Bakeries, Restaurants

review_info.review_text:
A friend got me interested in this coffee shop when she mentioned they sell vegan cookies. I'm vegan, and it's actually rather tough to find vegan sweet snacks in IV, so I was excited to try Campus Point. Funnily enough, when I headed over for a studying session, they were sold out! Guess those cookies must be good. 

However, the coconut charcoal mocha I tried was delicious--and vegan! Staff were super friendly, and I appreciated the indoor and outdoor seating options. Also, there's a toilet, which is a bonus, and the empanadas looked really yummy. I'll have to try one next time I visit.

Finally a place in Isla Vista with a bathroom!

Beyond the much needed bathroom this cafe offers a plethora of interesting drinks on top of providing all the basics. I got an iced charcoal mocha that was perfect to get rid of my mid afternoon slump.

This is such a cute lil coffee shop in the heart of IV. There's tables inside and outside to do work or chill, and sometimes there's an open mic outside. Prices are average for your local non-corporate coffee shop. The menu has a wide variety of drinks, and sometimes they post about BOGO's or 50% off on their instagram page. I'd definitely recommend this place to anyone who loves coffee and needs a place to study!

response: Mr B Restaurant & Cafe, located at 140 S Hope Ave, Ste A102 in Santa Barbara, CA, is a Middle Eastern restaurant and cafe open every day from 10 am to 8 pm. The restaurant is known for its Mediterranean food and also serves as a bakery. It offers amenities like free WiFi, outdoor seating, and is good for groups. The cafe also offers takeout services and has a parking lot. However, it does not offer valet, garage, or street parking. The ambiance is casual, not hipster or upscale. The restaurant has an overall rating of 4.0 stars, with customers praising the quality of food, particularly the shawarma and hummus. The staff has also been noted to be friendly. However, one reviewer expressed disappointment with the service, cleanliness, and some of the food offerings.

source_info.name: Mr B Restaurant & Cafe
source_info.categories: Food, Restaurants, Middle Eastern, Cafes, Bakeries

review_info.review_text: One of the best restaurants and Mediterranean food I've ever had!! They are very clean and the employees there are very nice and good energy as well as the owner.. I'm definitely going back. DEFINITELY RECOMMEND!!

Honestly i cant describe this place. It is one of my favorite stops in SB. The shawarma Sandwich taste sooo good and the hummus is the best. Alo the fried chicken was something else. Crispy and Halal. The staff are so friendly and the vibes are super. Will definitely come back. This place is super awesome.

We were excited to try this place out, but we're extremely disappointed. The service was slow, it seemed like there were only two people working. The silverware was not very clean. The ambiance was awkward, very quiet, like a doctors office lobby. The beef schwarma sandwich was not good at all. The meat had no flavor, couldn't taste any seasonings. It was also wrapped in a tortilla. A tortilla??! While the menu says "flat bread." I was hoping that at least the baklava would be good, but it was so dry. The hummus that came with the schwarma wasn't that great, either. The Turkish coffee was decent though. Would not recommend and will not come back. 1 star for being ambitious enough to open a new restaurant in town.

response:
Mr B Restaurant & Cafe is a Middle Eastern restaurant and cafe located at 140 S Hope Ave, Ste A102 in Santa Barbara, CA. The business offers a variety of food options, including Mediterranean cuisine, and serves as a bakery as well. The operating hours are from 10:00 AM to 8:00 PM every day of the week.

Customers have left mixed reviews about their experiences at Mr B Restaurant & Cafe. Some customers highly praise the establishment, describing it as one of the best restaurants they have ever visited. They appreciate the cleanliness, friendly staff, and good energy of the place. The Mediterranean food, particularly the shawarma sandwich and hummus, is highly recommended.

However, there is also a negative review highlighting various disappointments. The service was slow, possibly due to limited staff. The ambiance was described as awkward and quiet. The beef shawarma sandwich lacked flavor and was wrapped in a tortilla instead of flatbread as advertised. The baklava was dry, but the Turkish coffee was decent.

Overall, Mr B Restaurant & Cafe has a rating of 4.0 stars. While there are some areas for improvement, such as service and consistency in food quality, it is evident that the establishment has potential and offers delicious Middle Eastern cuisine.

response: Mr. B Restaurant & Cafe is a popular eatery located in Santa Barbara, California, offering a variety of Middle Eastern and Mediterranean dishes. The restaurant is known for its delicious Shawarma sandwiches, crispy falafel, and flavorful hummus. The atmosphere is casual and welcoming, with outdoor seating available. The staff is friendly and provides good service. The restaurant is well-liked by locals and visitors alike, with an average rating of 4.0 stars. Some reviewers have praised the restaurant's cleanliness, friendly staff, and tasty food, while others have criticized the slow service and lackluster ambiance. Free Wi-Fi is available, and the restaurant accepts reservations and takeout orders. Overall, Mr. B Restaurant & Cafe is a great option for those looking for tasty Middle Eastern cuisine in a casual setting.

source_info.name: Mr B Restaurant & Cafe
source_info.categories: Food, Restaurants, Middle Eastern, Cafes, Bakeries

review_info.review_text: One of the best restaurants and Mediterranean food I've ever had!! They are very clean and the employees there are very nice and good energy as well as the owner.. I'm definitely going back. DEFINITELY RECOMMEND!!

Honestly i cant describe this place. It is one of my favorite stops in SB. The shawarma Sandwich taste sooo good and the hummus is the best. Alo the fried chicken was something else. Crispy and Halal. The staff are so friendly and the vibes are super. Will definitely come back. This place is super awesome.

We were excited to try this place out, but we're extremely disappointed. The service was slow, it seemed like there were only two people working. The silverware was not very clean. The ambiance was awkward, very quiet, like a doctors office lobby. The beef schwarma sandwich was not good at all. The meat had no flavor, couldn't taste any seasonings. It was also wrapped in a tortilla. A tortilla??! While the menu says "flat bread." I was hoping that at least the baklava would be good, but it was so dry. The hummus that came with the schwarma wasn't that great, either. The Turkish coffee was decent though. Would not recommend and will not come back. 1 star for being ambitious enough to open a new restaurant in town.

response: Kogilicious, located at 956 Embarcadero Del Norte in Isla Vista, California, is a local favorite for Korean cuisine. The restaurant is open every day from 10:00 AM to 11:00 PM. While it does not offer WiFi or validated parking, it does provide street parking and outdoor seating. Catering to groups, it offers a casual ambience and is noted for its take-out service. The restaurant has a 4-star average rating. According to customer reviews, the half and half beef ribs and pork are described as the best some customers have ever had. The Kogilicious spicy chicken burrito is also a crowd favorite, along with the beef, spicy pork, and tofu options. Customers have praised the restaurant for its reasonable prices, especially when paying cash. However, it is noted that the use of a card may attract additional charges.

source_info.name: Kogilicious
source_info.categories: Korean, Restaurants

review_info.review_text: Half and half beef ribs and pork FTW.  Best I've *ever* had, and I've had a lot of good Korean food.

So. Good. So good. A Kogilicious spicy chicken burrito is one of my favorite foods in the world. But all of the proteins are fantastic- beef, spicy pork, tofu; can't go wrong. Taco, burrito, both are great. And so reasonably priced! Pay cash; you'll get a giant burrito for less than $7, which is unheard of elsewhere in IV. This town desperately needs a proper Korean restaurant, but in the meantime, Kogilicious hits the spot.

Three words, Spicy Pork Burrito.

Bomb.

Bring cash, they charge more if you use a card.

response: Dargan's Irish Pub & Restaurant is a popular gastropub located at 18 E Ortega St, Santa Barbara, CA. This establishment, noted for its relaxed, casual ambiance, operates from 11:30 AM to 2:00 AM throughout the week. It offers restaurant and bar services, with notable features including free WiFi, outdoor seating, reservations, take-out options, and group-friendly dining. Parking is available in a garage, but neither street parking, valet, nor lot parking is provided. The pub is also known for live music performances. Reviewers commend Dargan's for its excellent service, hearty food, and spacious, clean environment. It is particularly appreciated for its proper pint of Guinness, a rarity in the area. The pub has received a business star rating of 4.0, with recent reviews scoring it 4.0 to 5.0 stars.

source_info.name: Dargan's Irish Pub & Restaurant
source_info.categories: Restaurants, Bars, Irish, Nightlife, Pubs, Gastropubs

review_info.review_text:
Fun spot with multiple pool tables in the back. 

If you're local this is one of the spots where you will probably know most of the people inside on a busy Friday or Saturday night. 

Patty in the front is awesome as always.

We brought my mother-in-law for lunch.  She is English and we were relieved that she enjoyed the food.  The food was hearty and we were very full.  It's a very spacious and clean pub.  They also had outdoor dining.  We had very good service.

Finally, an Irish pub that can actually pour a proper pint of Guinness in southern California.  It is nice to see they actually have a legit nitro system, comparable to the ones from Ireland.  

This is a good place to grab a bite away from the hustle and bustle of state street.  There were plenty of available tables and no wait for dinner. 
This fish and chips were solid along with the steak pie.  Also had chicken tenders off the kids menu and my daughter had no complaints. 

4 stars.

response:
Dargan's Irish Pub & Restaurant is a popular local business located at 18 E Ortega St in Santa Barbara, CA. It is a restaurant and bar that offers Irish cuisine and a vibrant nightlife experience. The establishment operates from 11:30 am to 2:00 am every day of the week.

Customers have praised the pub for its friendly atmosphere and the presence of multiple pool tables in the back, providing a fun and enjoyable experience. It is considered a gathering place for locals, where many people are familiar with each other, especially on busy Friday or Saturday nights. Patty, who works at the front, has been commended for her exceptional service.

The pub offers outdoor seating and free WiFi for customers. They also have a reservation system in place, making it convenient for those who prefer to secure a table in advance. Moreover, Dargan's Irish Pub & Restaurant is known for its ability to pour a proper pint of Guinness, thanks to their authentic nitro system, which is comparable to those used in Ireland.

The menu at Dargan's includes hearty and satisfying dishes such as fish and chips, steak pie, and chicken tenders from the kids' menu. The availability of tables and the absence of wait times for dinner have been appreciated by visitors. Overall, the business has received a 4-star rating, indicating a positive customer experience.

response:
Dargan's Irish Pub & Restaurant is a popular spot in Santa Barbara, CA, offering a variety of dishes and drinks in a cozy and welcoming atmosphere. With a rating of 4.0 stars and over 70 reviews, it's clear that locals and visitors alike enjoy visiting this establishment.

The restaurant's menu features a mix of traditional Irish fare and American favorites, including fish and chips, shepherd's pie, burgers, and salads. According to reviews, the portions are generous, and the food is delicious, with one reviewer specifically mentioning that the fish and chips were "solid" and the steak pie was also satisfying.

In addition to its food, Dargan's is also known for its extensive selection of drinks, including beer, wine, and cocktails. Customers appreciate the fact that the pub has a legit nitro system, which allows them to pour a proper pint of Guinness, just like in Ireland.

The atmosphere at Dargan's is casual and comfortable, with both indoor and outdoor seating options. The interior is spacious and clean, with multiple pool tables in the back for entertainment. On warmer days, customers can enjoy the outdoor seating area, which is perfect for people-watching.

One of the standout aspects of Dargan's is the friendly and attentive service. Reviewers consistently mention that the staff is welcoming and helpful, with one reviewer praising Patty in particular for her excellent service.

While Dargan's is popular among locals, it's also a great spot for tourists looking for a authentic Irish experience. The pub's location on East Ortega Street puts it within walking distance of many hotels and other attractions in downtown Santa Barbara.

Overall, Dargan's Irish Pub & Restaurant is an excellent choice for anyone looking for a relaxed dining experience with a touch of Irish charm. Whether you're in the mood for traditional Irish cuisine, a cold drink, or just a fun atmosphere, Dargan's has something for everyone.

response: Uncorked Wine Tasting and Kitchen is a highly rated business located at 432 E Haley St, Santa Barbara, CA. It is categorized under Nightlife, Wine Bars, American (Traditional and New) Restaurants, Tapas/Small Plates, Wineries, and more. It operates from 5 PM to 7 PM on weekdays, while on Wednesdays, it is open from 11 AM to 3 PM. The establishment offers street parking, free WiFi, outdoor seating, and takeout services. However, it doesn't provide music or valet parking and doesn't take reservations. Its ambiance is described as hipster, trendy, classy, and casual. Despite having a business rating of 5 stars, reviews are mixed. Some customers praise the quality and taste of the food, particularly the prime rib and salmon wellington. However, there have been instances where the restaurant ran late on orders, once even running out of stuffing on a Thanksgiving dinner order, which led to customer dissatisfaction.

source_info.name: Uncorked Wine Tasting and Kitchen
source_info.categories: Nightlife, Wine Bars, Bars, American (Traditional), Tapas/Small Plates, Tapas Bars, Food, Arts & Entertainment, Wine Tasting Room, Wineries, American (New), Restaurants

review_info.review_text: We pre ordered our thanksgiving dinner. They ran over an hour late with our meal and ran out of stuffing. WTF... how do u run out of stuffing!!! I will never ever support this business!! Thank you for ruining our thanksgiving. Still waiting two hours laters... stop telling us 15 more minutes every time.

So glad to have discovered Uncorked!  Each meal we have ordered  for pick up has been fantastic,   with generous size portions and ready for our timed pickup.  Last night the prime rib was one of the best I have ever had with a delicious sauce, mashed potatoes, veggie salad and desert.

We have ordered take out from Uncorked many times over the last year - probably more than anywhere else. Every time we are thrilled with what we order. This most recent time we ordered the salmon wellington and beef wellington and were impressed by how tasty the puff pastry was, especially after traveling from the restaurant to our house. We'll be back often!

response: Neighbor Tim's BBQ is a popular food truck located at 152 -E Aero Camino, Goleta, CA, offering services in barbeque, food delivery, and American (new) cuisine. The business is open from Thursday to Sunday, between 16:45 and 20:00 and has a rating of 4.5 stars. Customers praise the quality of food, specifically mentioning the brisket, mac and cheese, and beans with bacon. Some customers felt that the food was a bit pricey and that the BBQ sauce was missing from some orders. The business also offers take-out but does not provide Wi-Fi or have any ambiance or music. Parking is available on the street. Despite some criticisms, most customers express a willingness to return, indicating overall satisfaction with the food and service.

source_info.name: Neighbor Tim's BBQ
source_info.categories: Food Trucks, Barbeque, Restaurants, Food Delivery Services, Food, American (New)

review_info.review_text:
I had been wanting to try Neighbor Tim's BBQ since I heard about it several months ago. Tonight was finally the night we were going to order it! I ordered through GrubHub - I got the 2 meats/1 side deal with 2 orders of brisket, Mac and cheese, and added a side of the potato salad with bacon, and the beans with bacon. That also comes with white bread, pickles, and their "famous BBQ sauce". Overall, it was a little pricey but IF it's good I am willing to pay it. 

So, I am really bummed out to report that I was really disappointed by the food. I wanted to like it so much I thought for sure this would be my new go-to BBQ place! But, one of the biggest things was they didn't give me any of their "famous BBQ sauce" and that's really what felt like was missing from the brisket. 

The sides were ok. I thought the Mac and cheese was a little dry/bland but with a little salt and pepper on it, it helped. I thought the potato salad with bacon was pretty good but needed something acidic to break down all the starchiness. The beans with bacon was good - probably my favorite side of the 3.
I'd be willing to give it another try in the future WITH their BBQ sauce though, so don't totally count me out!

Loved our family feast! Thank you for bringing the joy of smoked meats and carefully crafted sides to our Saturday night. Awesome brisket and mac and cheese. Glad we decided to make the last minute addition of corn and black bean salad. Amazing customer service.  Thank you Tim's crew! We'll be ordering again.

One of the good things about having grown up nephews and nieces: people of whom i have had a good amount of contact and influence on when they were growing up: adults now who drive, think, analyze, care about you (as in me) is that i can ask them to drive me somewhere, get me something at various and sundry places and as in last week: order, pay for and pick up a meal. To be fair i warned them when they were kids: your uncle is going to expect to be paid back for all the driving, picking up, leaving off, etc., etc., i did for you. So NOW is that time.
But for now i am relishing the well earned attention. And i'm only 1/2 kidding here in any event.
Several weeks ago my niece told me about Neighbor Tim's both as a food truck and a stand alone BBQ joint.
I zipped into SB and asked her to bring some BBQ for me, her kids and hubby as well as for my sisters. She raved about this place. So i thought: ok, sounds good: so bring me some, please.
Well bring me some she did:
Pork ribs
Brisket
Potato Salad
Mac and cheese
White Bread thick sliced
Sauce: Amazing Sauce it is called
Burnt Ends (from another time but reviewed here)
The ribs were perfectly cooked and meaty. I am a purist when it comes to ribs. they must be tender and juicy but not falling off the bone meaning i pick one up and the meat falls off. That is too well cooked, usually cooked with too much moisture and not enough heat or smoke.
These ribs though were well smoked, well seasoned (not too salty) and were delivered w/o sauce which is the way i prefer them so that you can taste the meat and not just the sauce. The sauce btw is super also: vinegary, light on the sugars with a snappy chili finish: north carolina style which surprised me only because i thought this was a Texas style BBQ joint. But it works so why fight it. 
The Brisket was also well seasoned and smoked as well as very moist and tender. No knife needed to cut and we made sammys using the white bread and dipping in the sauce to finish.
The Mac and cheese is nothing special: just cheese sauce and noodles. This needed salt and pepper for starters. 
The potato salad was a big letdown also: the dressing was good and in the right proportion: neither too much or not enough but again salt and pepper would have helped and acid is needed desperately. Pickles, mustard, maybe or both?  Also most damning is that the potatoes were slightly undercooked. in other words too al dente for my taste. 
Thick white bread was in the Texas toast style and it was real tasty: reminded me of a traditional pullman loaf. Yeasty, high quality bread not Wonder Bread, remember?
The burnt ends were not part of this order but we had these about 4 weeks ago and they were absolutely first rate: charred, and well seasoned to within an inch of their lives. Tim's are made from the deckle of the brisket i am going to assume and are super tasty chunks of beefy goodness. Next day you can thinly slice and use in a taco or sammy or just to pop in your mouth while pretending to grab a beer from the refer.
All in all a very good sampling today of Neighbor Tim's wares:  what they do well they do very, very well. I will be back!

music

response:
Neighbor Tim's BBQ is a local business located at 152 -E Aero Camino in Goleta, CA. They specialize in barbecue and offer food delivery services through GrubHub. The business is open on Thursday, Friday, Saturday, and Sunday from 16:45 to 20:00. The business does not have a garage for parking, but street parking is available. They do not offer outdoor seating or WiFi. However, they do provide takeout services. The business has an average rating of 4.5 stars.

Based on customer reviews, the food at Neighbor Tim's BBQ receives mixed feedback. One customer was disappointed with their order, as they did not receive the "famous BBQ sauce" that they were looking forward to. They found the sides to be okay, with the mac and cheese being a little dry and bland. However, they enjoyed the beans with bacon the most. Despite their disappointment, they mentioned that they would be willing to give the business another try with the BBQ sauce.

Another customer had a positive experience, praising the quality of the brisket and mac and cheese. They also appreciated the customer service and decided to add the corn and black bean salad to their order. The ribs were described as perfectly cooked and well-seasoned, while the brisket was moist and tender. The customer found the sauce to be vinegary with a chili finish, resembling North Carolina style BBQ.

Overall, Neighbor Tim's BBQ offers a variety of barbecue dishes with a range of flavors. While some customers have been disappointed with certain aspects of their orders, others have enjoyed the quality of the meats and sides.

source_info.name: Neighbor Tim's BBQ
source_info.categories: Food Trucks, Barbeque, Restaurants, Food Delivery Services, Food, American (New)

review_info.review_text:
I had been wanting to try Neighbor Tim's BBQ since I heard about it several months ago. Tonight was finally the night we were going to order it! I ordered through GrubHub - I got the 2 meats/1 side deal with 2 orders of brisket, Mac and cheese, and added a side of the potato salad with bacon, and the beans with bacon. That also comes with white bread, pickles, and their "famous BBQ sauce". Overall, it was a little pricey but IF it's good I am willing to pay it. 

So, I am really bummed out to report that I was really disappointed by the food. I wanted to like it so much I thought for sure this would be my new go-to BBQ place! But, one of the biggest things was they didn't give me any of their "famous BBQ sauce" and that's really what felt like was missing from the brisket. 

The sides were ok. I thought the Mac and cheese was a little dry/bland but with a little salt and pepper on it, it helped. I thought the potato salad with bacon was pretty good but needed something acidic to break down all the starchiness. The beans with bacon was good - probably my favorite side of the 3.
I'd be willing to give it another try in the future WITH their BBQ sauce though, so don't totally count me out!

Loved our family feast! Thank you for bringing the joy of smoked meats and carefully crafted sides to our Saturday night. Awesome brisket and mac and cheese. Glad we decided to make the last minute addition of corn and black bean salad. Amazing customer service.  Thank you Tim's crew! We'll be ordering again.

One of the good things about having grown up nephews and nieces: people of whom i have had a good amount of contact and influence on when they were growing up: adults now who drive, think, analyze, care about you (as in me) is that i can ask them to drive me somewhere, get me something at various and sundry places and as in last week: order, pay for and pick up a meal. To be fair i warned them when they were kids: your uncle is going to expect to be paid back for all the driving, picking up, leaving off, etc., etc., i did for you. So NOW is that time.
But for now i am relishing the well earned attention. And i'm only 1/2 kidding here in any event.
Several weeks ago my niece told me about Neighbor Tim's both as a food truck and a stand alone BBQ joint.
I zipped into SB and asked her to bring some BBQ for me, her kids and hubby as well as for my sisters. She raved about this place. So i thought: ok, sounds good: so bring me some, please.
Well bring me some she did:
Pork ribs
Brisket
Potato Salad
Mac and cheese
White Bread thick sliced
Sauce: Amazing Sauce it is called
Burnt Ends (from another time but reviewed here)
The ribs were perfectly cooked and meaty. I am a purist when it comes to ribs. they must be tender and juicy but not falling off the bone meaning i pick one up and the meat falls off. That is too well cooked, usually cooked with too much moisture and not enough heat or smoke.
These ribs though were well smoked, well seasoned (not too salty) and were delivered w/o sauce which is the way i prefer them so that you can taste the meat and not just the sauce. The sauce btw is super also: vinegary, light on the sugars with a snappy chili finish: north carolina style which surprised me only because i thought this was a Texas style BBQ joint. But it works so why fight it. 
The Brisket was also well seasoned and smoked as well as very moist and tender. No knife needed to cut and we made sammys using the white bread and dipping in the sauce to finish.
The Mac and cheese is nothing special: just cheese sauce and noodles. This needed salt and pepper for starters. 
The potato salad was a big letdown also: the dressing was good and in the right proportion: neither too much or not enough but again salt and pepper would have helped and acid is needed desperately. Pickles, mustard, maybe or both?  Also most damning is that the potatoes were slightly undercooked. in other words too al dente for my taste. 
Thick white bread was in the Texas toast style and it was real tasty: reminded me of a traditional pullman loaf. Yeasty, high quality bread not Wonder Bread, remember?
The burnt ends were not part of this order but we had these about 4 weeks ago and they were absolutely first rate: charred, and well seasoned to within an inch of their lives. Tim's are made from the deckle of the brisket i am going to assume and are super tasty chunks of beefy goodness. Next day you can thinly slice and use in a taco or sammy or just to pop in your mouth while pretending to grab a beer from the refer.
All in all a very good sampling today of Neighbor Tim's wares:  what they do well they do very, very well. I will be back!

pred: The business does not have a garage for parking, but street parking is available.
They do not offer outdoor seating or WiFi.
The ribs were described as perfectly cooked and well-seasoned, while the brisket was moist and tender.
The customer found the sauce to be vinegary with a chili finish, resembling North Carolina style BBQ.

response: Neighbor Tim's BBQ is a food truck located in Goleta, California. The business serves barbecue, American cuisine, food delivery services, food trucks, and restaurants. Customers can order online through GrubHub, and the restaurant offers outdoor seating. The restaurant takes out orders and is good for groups. The menu includes brisket, potato salad, beans with bacon, and mac and cheese, among others. The restaurant has a four-point-five-star rating, and customers have left reviews with varying experiences. One customer praised the brisket and mac and cheese but found the potato salad lacking. Another customer appreciated the excellent customer service and highly recommended the corn and black bean salad. Overall, the business appears to offer a variety of barbecue options and a decent selection of sides, but customers may need to adjust the seasoning to their liking.

response: Neighbor Tim's BBQ is a popular food truck and restaurant located in Goleta, California, serving delicious barbecue and Southern-style cuisine. According to customer reviews, the restaurant is known for its slow-smoked meats, particularly the brisket and pork ribs, which are perfectly cooked and flavorful. The menu offers a variety of options, including mac and cheese, potato salad, and white bread, all of which are made with high-quality ingredients. Customers have praised the restaurant's customer service, with one reviewer stating that the staff is friendly and accommodating. However, some customers have noted that the sides, such as the potato salad and mac and cheese, could benefit from more acidity and seasoning. Nonetheless, Neighbor Tim's BBQ has received positive reviews for its tasty and authentic barbecue dishes.

response:
Here's an objective overview of Neighbor Tim's BBQ based on the provided structured data:

Neighbor Tim's BBQ is a food truck and restaurant located in Goleta, California that serves American (New) cuisine, specifically barbecue. They offer food delivery services and have outdoor seating, but no WiFi. Their hours of operation are Thursday to Sunday from 4:45 PM to 8:00 PM. They have a rating of 4.5 stars based on three reviews.

One reviewer was disappointed with their experience, stating that the food was overpriced and lacking the "famous BBQ sauce" that they were expecting. However, two other reviewers had positive experiences, praising the brisket, mac and cheese, and burnt ends. One reviewer noted that the potato salad was a letdown and suggested adding salt, pepper, and acid to improve it.

Overall, Neighbor Tim's BBQ seems to have mixed reviews, with some customers enjoying their dishes and others feeling disappointed by certain aspects of their meals.

source_info.name: Neighbor Tim's BBQ
source_info.categories: Food Trucks, Barbeque, Restaurants, Food Delivery Services, Food, American (New)

review_info.review_text:
I had been wanting to try Neighbor Tim's BBQ since I heard about it several months ago. Tonight was finally the night we were going to order it! I ordered through GrubHub - I got the 2 meats/1 side deal with 2 orders of brisket, Mac and cheese, and added a side of the potato salad with bacon, and the beans with bacon. That also comes with white bread, pickles, and their "famous BBQ sauce". Overall, it was a little pricey but IF it's good I am willing to pay it. 

So, I am really bummed out to report that I was really disappointed by the food. I wanted to like it so much I thought for sure this would be my new go-to BBQ place! But, one of the biggest things was they didn't give me any of their "famous BBQ sauce" and that's really what felt like was missing from the brisket. 

The sides were ok. I thought the Mac and cheese was a little dry/bland but with a little salt and pepper on it, it helped. I thought the potato salad with bacon was pretty good but needed something acidic to break down all the starchiness. The beans with bacon was good - probably my favorite side of the 3.
I'd be willing to give it another try in the future WITH their BBQ sauce though, so don't totally count me out!

Loved our family feast! Thank you for bringing the joy of smoked meats and carefully crafted sides to our Saturday night. Awesome brisket and mac and cheese. Glad we decided to make the last minute addition of corn and black bean salad. Amazing customer service.  Thank you Tim's crew! We'll be ordering again.

One of the good things about having grown up nephews and nieces: people of whom i have had a good amount of contact and influence on when they were growing up: adults now who drive, think, analyze, care about you (as in me) is that i can ask them to drive me somewhere, get me something at various and sundry places and as in last week: order, pay for and pick up a meal. To be fair i warned them when they were kids: your uncle is going to expect to be paid back for all the driving, picking up, leaving off, etc., etc., i did for you. So NOW is that time.
But for now i am relishing the well earned attention. And i'm only 1/2 kidding here in any event.
Several weeks ago my niece told me about Neighbor Tim's both as a food truck and a stand alone BBQ joint.
I zipped into SB and asked her to bring some BBQ for me, her kids and hubby as well as for my sisters. She raved about this place. So i thought: ok, sounds good: so bring me some, please.
Well bring me some she did:
Pork ribs
Brisket
Potato Salad
Mac and cheese
White Bread thick sliced
Sauce: Amazing Sauce it is called
Burnt Ends (from another time but reviewed here)
The ribs were perfectly cooked and meaty. I am a purist when it comes to ribs. they must be tender and juicy but not falling off the bone meaning i pick one up and the meat falls off. That is too well cooked, usually cooked with too much moisture and not enough heat or smoke.
These ribs though were well smoked, well seasoned (not too salty) and were delivered w/o sauce which is the way i prefer them so that you can taste the meat and not just the sauce. The sauce btw is super also: vinegary, light on the sugars with a snappy chili finish: north carolina style which surprised me only because i thought this was a Texas style BBQ joint. But it works so why fight it. 
The Brisket was also well seasoned and smoked as well as very moist and tender. No knife needed to cut and we made sammys using the white bread and dipping in the sauce to finish.
The Mac and cheese is nothing special: just cheese sauce and noodles. This needed salt and pepper for starters. 
The potato salad was a big letdown also: the dressing was good and in the right proportion: neither too much or not enough but again salt and pepper would have helped and acid is needed desperately. Pickles, mustard, maybe or both?  Also most damning is that the potatoes were slightly undercooked. in other words too al dente for my taste. 
Thick white bread was in the Texas toast style and it was real tasty: reminded me of a traditional pullman loaf. Yeasty, high quality bread not Wonder Bread, remember?
The burnt ends were not part of this order but we had these about 4 weeks ago and they were absolutely first rate: charred, and well seasoned to within an inch of their lives. Tim's are made from the deckle of the brisket i am going to assume and are super tasty chunks of beefy goodness. Next day you can thinly slice and use in a taco or sammy or just to pop in your mouth while pretending to grab a beer from the refer.
All in all a very good sampling today of Neighbor Tim's wares:  what they do well they do very, very well. I will be back!

response:
Neighbor Tim's BBQ is a popular food truck and restaurant in Goleta, CA, known for its delicious barbecue dishes and friendly service. The menu features a variety of meats, including brisket, pork ribs, and burnt ends, as well as sides such as mac and cheese, potato salad, and white bread. The restaurant has a casual, rustic atmosphere and offers take-out and delivery options.

According to customer reviews, Neighbor Tim's BBQ serves high-quality meats that are smoked to perfection and seasoned with a perfect balance of flavors. The brisket and ribs are particularly popular among customers, who praise their tenderness and rich flavor. The mac and cheese and potato salad are also well-liked, although some customers suggest adding more seasoning to the latter.

One customer notes that the burnt ends are not part of the regular menu but are absolutely worth trying. They are made from the deckle of the brisket and are described as "super tasty chunks of beefy goodness." Another customer mentions that the white bread is a highlight, comparing it to a traditional pullman loaf.

While some customers have noted that the service can be slow, the staff is generally praised for their friendliness and attentiveness. One reviewer mentions that the crew provides excellent customer service and thank them for bringing the joy of smoked meats and carefully crafted sides to their Saturday night.

In summary, Neighbor Tim's BBQ is a great spot for barbecue lovers in Goleta, offering delicious meats, tasty sides, and a cozy atmosphere. While there may be some issues with service speed, the staff is generally friendly and attentive, making it a great choice for a casual dinner or gathering with friends and family.

source_info.name: Neighbor Tim's BBQ
source_info.categories: Food Trucks, Barbeque, Restaurants, Food Delivery Services, Food, American (New)

review_info.review_text:
I had been wanting to try Neighbor Tim's BBQ since I heard about it several months ago. Tonight was finally the night we were going to order it! I ordered through GrubHub - I got the 2 meats/1 side deal with 2 orders of brisket, Mac and cheese, and added a side of the potato salad with bacon, and the beans with bacon. That also comes with white bread, pickles, and their "famous BBQ sauce". Overall, it was a little pricey but IF it's good I am willing to pay it. 

So, I am really bummed out to report that I was really disappointed by the food. I wanted to like it so much I thought for sure this would be my new go-to BBQ place! But, one of the biggest things was they didn't give me any of their "famous BBQ sauce" and that's really what felt like was missing from the brisket. 

The sides were ok. I thought the Mac and cheese was a little dry/bland but with a little salt and pepper on it, it helped. I thought the potato salad with bacon was pretty good but needed something acidic to break down all the starchiness. The beans with bacon was good - probably my favorite side of the 3.
I'd be willing to give it another try in the future WITH their BBQ sauce though, so don't totally count me out!

Loved our family feast! Thank you for bringing the joy of smoked meats and carefully crafted sides to our Saturday night. Awesome brisket and mac and cheese. Glad we decided to make the last minute addition of corn and black bean salad. Amazing customer service.  Thank you Tim's crew! We'll be ordering again.

One of the good things about having grown up nephews and nieces: people of whom i have had a good amount of contact and influence on when they were growing up: adults now who drive, think, analyze, care about you (as in me) is that i can ask them to drive me somewhere, get me something at various and sundry places and as in last week: order, pay for and pick up a meal. Mac and cheese
White Bread thick sliced
Sauce: Amazing Sauce it is called
Burnt Ends (from another time but reviewed here)

These ribs though were well smoked, well seasoned (not too salty) and were delivered w/o sauce which is the way i prefer them so that you can taste the meat and not just the sauce. The sauce btw is super also: vinegary, light on the sugars with a snappy chili finish: north carolina style which surprised me only because i thought this was a Texas style BBQ joint. But it works so why fight it. 
The Brisket was also well seasoned and smoked as well as very moist and tender. No knife needed to cut and we made sammys using the white bread and dipping in the sauce to finish.
The Mac and cheese is nothing special: just cheese sauce and noodles. This needed salt and pepper for starters. 
The potato salad was a big letdown also: the dressing was good and in the right proportion: neither too much or not enough but again salt and pepper would have helped and acid is needed desperately. Pickles, mustard, maybe or both?  Also most damning is that the potatoes were slightly undercooked. in other words too al dente for my taste. 
Thick white bread was in the Texas toast style and it was real tasty: reminded me of a traditional pullman loaf. Yeasty, high quality bread not Wonder Bread, remember?
The burnt ends were not part of this order but we had these about 4 weeks ago and they were absolutely first rate: charred, and well seasoned to within an inch of their lives. Tim's are made from the deckle of the brisket i am going to assume and are super tasty chunks of beefy goodness. Next day you can thinly slice and use in a taco or sammy or just to pop in your mouth while pretending to grab a beer from the refer.
All in all a very good sampling today of Neighbor Tim's wares:  what they do well they do very, very well. I will be back!

response: California Wood-Fired Catering, located in Santa Barbara, CA, is a local business with a 4.5-star rating that specializes in food truck services, event planning, pizza, catering, and more. While the business does not offer reservations or takeout services, it is well-suited for accommodating groups. Reviews from customers highlight their ability to cater to large groups, with one event feeding about 100 people with pizza, salad, and gelato. Their chefs are noted for working hard to please the crowd and doing a great job under pressure. The food is praised as great, delicious and a hit at events. However, some reviews point out minor issues, like running out of salad a bit early and a slow response rate via email. Despite these minor obstacles, the overall customer experience is reported as positive, with many clients expressing pleasure in working with California Wood-Fired Catering.

source_info.name: California Wood-Fired Catering
source_info.categories: Food Trucks, Event Planning & Services, Pizza, Restaurants, Caterers, Food

review_info.review_text:
We had them provide pizza for about 100 folks at Municipal Wine. Food was great -- salad, pizza, and gelato. All great. 

They warned us that it would be hard to get pizzas out for that large a crowd, and they were right. But they did the best they could in the situation and everyone was happy. Their chefs worked hard to please and did a great job. 

Only negatives were: underestimated salad, so we ran out a little early. And Email response was a little slow. That said, a pleasure to work with and tasty food!

California Wood-Fired Catering is delicious and perfect for a casual, fun event! I have had the pleasure of working with them many times for events at the private event venue that I manage. While I have heard from a few clients that the owner can be sometimes difficult to get a hold of, I myself have never had a problem reaching him by calling the number listed on their website. Either way they always come through with the events that they book and the guests always rave about how fun it is having a wood-fired burning oven popping out fresh pizzas as well as how delicious the food is.

This year for 4th of July we checked out the Stow House old fashioned 4th of July:) What a pleasant surprise. We were lucky enough to stumble on these pizza people who were making pizza in special oven:) it was really good and hope they can come to our school carnivals next year:)

Only negatives were: underestimated salad, so we ran out a little early. And Email response was a little slow. That said, a pleasure to work with and tasty food!

California Wood-Fired Catering is delicious and perfect for a casual, fun event! I have had the pleasure of working with them many times for events at the private event venue that I manage. While I have heard from a few clients that the owner can be sometimes difficult to get a hold of, I myself have never had a problem reaching him by calling the number listed on their website. Either way they always come through with the events that they book and the guests always rave about how fun it is having a wood-fired burning oven popping out fresh pizzas as well as how delicious the food is.

This year for 4th of July we checked out the Stow House old fashioned 4th of July:) What a pleasant surprise. We were lucky enough to stumble on these pizza people who were making pizza in special oven:) it was really good and hope they can come to our school carnivals next year:)

response: Sushi Ai is a Japanese restaurant specializing in sushi, located at 7020 Marketplace Dr, Goleta, CA. The restaurant operates mainly from 11:00 to 21:00, with extended hours till 21:30 on Fridays and Saturdays. It offers street parking, outdoor seating, free WiFi, and is good for groups. The ambiance is casual, and they offer takeout services. However, they don't take reservations. The restaurant has received mixed reviews, with a business rating of 3.0 stars. Some customers praised the polite staff, cleanliness, and freshness of the food. They also commended the restaurant's delivery service. However, other reviews mentioned issues with incorrect orders, under-staffing during rush hours, and overpriced, subpar sushi.

source_info.name: Sushi Ai
source_info.categories: Sushi Bars, Restaurants, Japanese

review_info.review_text:
Whenever I order at Sushi Ai
The entire staff is polite, and accommodating. The restaurant is very clean. I
Have been a customer since they opened... 
Now that there are deliveries
This restaurant, and doordash do a great job. The sushi, and tempura, and soups...everything so fresh So Good. Thank you Sushi Ai, especially Leanna P. S.
Sauces Teri, eel, spicy mayo!
Yum yum

Oye my fiance and I went here today for the first and last time.
I felt bad for the girls trying to run the dinner rush because they were so overwhelmed. There was only two people in front.
We ordered two bento boxes and edamame. We got the boxes fast but my fiances was wrong. The girl took it back without giving me any utensils. We finally got my utensils and his food and she walked away without giving him utensils.
We also purchased edamame, reminded the girl and still never got it. (We did get a refund for that).
But while I was waiting for 10 minutes for the refund, 1 person came up to say 3 things were missing for their order and another woman came up saying her teriyaki was cold. There was also a ridiculous amount of togo orders that they were super behind on.

The manager/ owner needs to pay more people to work. I dont know if someone just called out or they are extremely under staffed but be careful coming here on a rush.

My sushi was served hot...I think that's enough said.

I ordered the dynamite roll and it just wasn't good. I understand this is americanized sushi but the scallops were overcooked and it was covered in a heavy layer of spicy mayo. For $15, it's really not worth it. Spend your money elsewhere.

response:
Sushi Ai is a local business located at 7020 Marketplace Dr in Goleta, CA. It is a sushi bar and restaurant that offers Japanese cuisine. The restaurant is open from Tuesday to Sunday, with varying hours of operation. Customers can enjoy their meal in the outdoor seating area, and there is free WiFi available for guests. Sushi Ai also offers takeout services and can accommodate large groups.

According to a recent customer review, the staff at Sushi Ai is polite and accommodating. The restaurant is known for its cleanliness and fresh food, including sushi, tempura, and soups. The reviewer expresses gratitude towards the restaurant and mentions specific sauces they enjoyed. This positive review indicates a satisfactory dining experience.

However, another customer had a negative experience during their visit. They felt that the restaurant was understaffed and overwhelmed during the dinner rush. The customer encountered issues with their order, including incorrect food and missing utensils. They also noted that other customers had complaints about missing items and cold food. This review highlights potential shortcomings in terms of staffing and order accuracy.

In a separate review, a customer expressed disappointment with their sushi, specifically mentioning overcooked scallops and an excessive amount of spicy mayo. They felt that the quality did not justify the price and suggested exploring other options.

Overall, Sushi Ai has received mixed reviews, with some customers praising the cleanliness, friendly staff, and fresh food, while others have expressed concerns about staffing and the quality of certain menu items.

I felt bad for the girls trying to run the dinner rush because they were so overwhelmed. There was only two people in front.
We ordered two bento boxes and edamame. We got the boxes fast but my fiances was wrong. The girl took it back without giving me any utensils. We finally got my utensils and his food and she walked away without giving him utensils.
We also purchased edamame, reminded the girl and still never got it. (We did get a refund for that).
But while I was waiting for 10 minutes for the refund, 1 person came up to say 3 things were missing for their order and another woman came up saying her teriyaki was cold. There was also a ridiculous amount of togo orders that they were super behind on.

The manager/ owner needs to pay more people to work. I dont know if someone just called out or they are extremely under staffed but be careful coming here on a rush.

My sushi was served hot...I think that's enough said.

I ordered the dynamite roll and it just wasn't good. I understand this is americanized sushi but the scallops were overcooked and it was covered in a heavy layer of spicy mayo. For $15, it's really not worth it. Spend your money elsewhere.

response: Minnow Cafe, located at 9 Breakwater in Santa Barbara, CA, is a casual eatery categorized under Restaurants and Delis. It boasts a 4-star rating for its services. The reviews highlight its prime location near the harbor, perfect for enjoying their fresh seafood offerings, including praised dishes like clam chowder and fish and chips. Also noted is the convenience of its fast-food setup, which allows customers to order and enjoy their meals quickly, ideal for those exploring the city. However, some reviews indicate that the food might be seen as expensive for a fast-food experience and mention a slightly less satisfactory customer service. The cafe offers outdoor seating and take-out services, but does not offer WiFi, music, or reservations. It also lacks business parking except for a lot.

source_info.name: Minnow Cafe
source_info.categories: Restaurants, Delis

review_info.review_text:
This place is perfect to get some seafood in Santa Barbara! I was glad to be able to find this hole in a wall while walking with my group for lunch. 

I ordered the clam chowder bread bowl and fish and chips. Clam chowder was very delicious and fresh. The fish was crispy and made into perfection. 

The best part is that you can order the food right away and simply sit at the tables outside to eat near the harbor where the ocean is so close. So if you want to get food in a quickly manner in order to have more time to explore the other areas in Santa Barbara, I would highly recommend Minnow Cafe.
Hard to beat for a fast-food-type breakfast or lunch at the marina. Simple, good food, well prepared.

I really like the Minnow...I mean you can't beat the location for a fast food establishment and the Fish Tacos are pretty good...BUT I'm always horrified to discover how much this fast food experience costs.  I mean, it's really not very good food unless you view it as cheap (but it's not).  Great thing, sitting outside by the harbor watching the BPs go by while you eat cheap (seeming) food.  Bad part, greasy spoon food served on paper plates by snarky staff with condiments that blow away...but it ain't cheap.  Guess that's Santa Barbara.

response:
The India Club is a local restaurant located at 5701 Calle Real in Goleta, CA. It specializes in Indian cuisine and is known for its good food and friendly staff. The restaurant offers free WiFi and outdoor seating, making it a great place to dine and relax. It is open from 16:30 to 21:00 on weekdays and until 21:30 on Fridays and Saturdays.

Customers have left mixed reviews about The India Club. Some have praised the food, describing it as fresh and delicious. The lunch buffet and dinner options have been particularly appreciated. There is also a small salad bar, which seems to be a favorite among the customers. However, there have been a few negative reviews expressing disappointment about the sudden closure of the restaurant without any notice to the customers. This has left some patrons unsure about the future of the business and whether it will reopen.

Overall, The India Club has received a rating of 3.5 stars. Despite the closure concerns, it seems to have a loyal customer base who appreciate the quality of the food and the pleasant dining experience.

review_info.review_text: noooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooo  they are closed .I am so sad. Their food was really really good and am really hoping they will relocate maybe. I did find out though the family also runs NAAN  STOP (get it haha) in IV so I will check it out. The food here was really good,fresh ,the people were nice,chai tea good,lunch buffet and dinner was good and they had a little salad bar my favorite part.Santa Barbara maybe upped the rent and drove them out just like all great places here

Loved this place but am disappointed (just like others) about its sudden disappearance with NO notice to customers.  Are you temporarily closed?  Closed forever?  How can a business do that without letting customers know?  If they are closed temporarily, it's not a good way to keep a customer coming back.  I don't want to drive over there multiple times to see if they are open or closed.  Very disappointed.
Went for dinner on 7/12/16. It looks like the restaurant is closing. So sad. I had a hunkering for good Indian food.
